2. Key requirements defined
3. Domain model and constraints
4. Integration requirements
5. Any gaps or unclear areas in the specs

Read all spec files completely and summarize what the system is supposed to do.

content.text: I'll conduct a thorough review of the specs directory to understand the system requirements, domain model, and constraints.
Now let me read all the specification files in order:

content.content:
1тЖТ# Metro Fleet Mileage Management System
     2тЖТ# Part 1: Executive Summary & Decision Record
     3тЖТ
     4тЖТ**Document Version:** 2.1  
     5тЖТ**Status:** APPROVED FOR DEVELOPMENT  
     6тЖТ**Last Updated:** 2026-01-19
     7тЖТ
     8тЖТ---
     9тЖТ
    10тЖТ## 1.1 System Purpose
    11тЖТ
    12тЖТThe Metro Fleet Mileage Management System (MMS) is a constraint-based fleet management platform that optimizes train deployments and maintenance scheduling over a 12-year contract period. It replaces manual Excel-based planning with an integrated system providing:
    13тЖТ
    14тЖТ1. **Daily mileage tracking** with automated ingestion from CSV uploads
    15тЖТ2. **Run assignment optimization** balancing mileage across the fleet
    16тЖТ3. **Preventive maintenance scheduling** within resource constraints
    17тЖТ4. **Strategic planning** for 1-10 year horizons to prevent "bow wave" accumulation
    18тЖТ5. **Man-hour leveling** to optimize workforce utilization
    19тЖТ6. **Cost forecasting** with TCO projection and variance analysis
    20тЖТ7. **Simulation capabilities** for what-if scenario planning
    21тЖТ8. **Unified visualization** via an Infinite Canvas interface
    22тЖТ9. **Service demand validation** ensuring fleet availability meets operational requirements
    23тЖТ10. **Unavailability awareness** integrating corrective maintenance status for schedule validity
    24тЖТ
    25тЖТ---
    26тЖТ
    27тЖТ## 1.2 Project Scope
    28тЖТ
    29тЖТ### 1.2.1 In Scope (MMS Manages)
    30тЖТ
    31тЖТ| Capability | Description |
    32тЖТ|------------|-------------|
    33тЖТ| Fleet roster management | Trains, cars, components with lifecycle tracking |
    34тЖТ| Preventive Maintenance scheduling | C1, C2, C3, C4, Overhaul planning with constraint validation |
    35тЖТ| Run assignment optimization | Daily route allocation with mileage balancing |
    36тЖТ| Mileage projection and tracking | Route-based utilization profiles, 12-year forecasting |
    37тЖТ| Constraint validation | Track capacity, labor, materials, unavailability |
    38тЖТ| Maximo integration | Bidirectional sync for PM work orders |
    39тЖТ| Multi-year capital planning | Bow-wave detection, cost smoothing |
    40тЖТ| Service demand validation | Ensuring minimum fleet availability targets |
    41тЖТ
    42тЖТ### 1.2.2 In Scope (MMS Must Receive/React To)
    43тЖТ
    44тЖТ| External Data | Source | Purpose |
    45тЖТ|---------------|--------|---------|
    46тЖТ| Corrective maintenance status | Maximo webhook/polling | Block unavailable trains from scheduling |
    47тЖТ| Inventory snapshots | ERP/Maximo | Validate material constraints |
    48тЖТ| Service demand requirements | Operations team | Set minimum availability targets |
    49тЖТ
    50тЖТ### 1.2.3 Out of Scope (MMS Does Not Execute)
    51тЖТ
    52тЖТ| Capability | Owner System | MMS Interaction |
    53тЖТ|------------|--------------|-----------------|
    54тЖТ| Corrective maintenance work orders | Maximo CMMS | Receives status updates only |
    55тЖТ| Parts inventory management | ERP/Maximo | Consumes snapshots for constraint validation |
    56тЖТ| Operator scheduling | Workforce management | None |
    57тЖТ| Real-time GPS tracking | OCC systems | None |
    58тЖТ
    59тЖТ> **Critical Clarification:** "Out of Scope" means MMS does not *manage* these functions. However, MMS **must receive** corrective maintenance status to prevent scheduling conflicts. Without this visibility, every PM schedule is potentially invalid on the day of execution.
    60тЖТ
    61тЖТ---
    62тЖТ
    63тЖТ## 1.3 Key Architectural Decisions
    64тЖТ
    65тЖТ| ID | Decision | Rationale | Alternatives Considered | Risk Mitigation |
    66тЖТ|----|----------|-----------|------------------------|-----------------|
    67тЖТ| ADR-001 | TypeScript-First Architecture | Team expertise, single language for 98% of codebase, strong typing | Java (enterprise), Python (data science) | Node.js fallback path documented |
    68тЖТ| ADR-002 | C++ Native Binary for Tactical Optimization | Container density (20-30 pods/node), <5ms cold start, OR-Tools native performance | WASM solver (limited), Pure TS (10x slower) | TypeScript fallback solver, Docker build pipeline |
    69тЖТ| ADR-003 | Dual-Horizon Planning (Strategic + Tactical) | RFQ requires 1-10 year planning; single solver cannot handle both granularities | Single solver (bow wave risk), Manual strategic planning | Constraint aggregation layer ensures consistency |
    70тЖТ| ADR-004 | Soft Constraints with Explainability | Operations cannot accept "INFEASIBLE"; must provide actionable least-bad solutions | Hard constraints only (operationally unacceptable) | Fallback greedy solver, explanation trace |
    71тЖТ| ADR-005 | Material Constraints in Optimizer | PM scheduling without parts availability is operationally useless | Labor-only optimization (cited in reviews as critical gap) | Inventory snapshot integration, reservation system |
    72тЖТ| ADR-006 | Baseline/Drift Architecture | Long-term plans cannot recalculate daily; planners need stable reference points | Continuous recalculation (jittery UX, trust erosion) | Published baselines, drift thresholds, dampening |
    73тЖТ| ADR-007 | Scenario-Based Simulation | RFQ requires what-if analysis; cannot store in single-reality schema | In-memory only (data loss), Full DB clone (storage bloat) | Event-sourced deltas, materialized comparison views |
    74тЖТ| ADR-008 | Infinite Canvas UI (deck.gl) | Semantic zoom from 12-year strategy to daily operations without context switching | Paginated tables (poor UX), Separate views (fragmented) | Progressive enhancement, tabular fallback |
    75тЖТ| ADR-009 | Explanation Trace in Optimizer Output | Operators must understand "why" to trust and adopt system recommendations | Black-box output (user rejection, manual overrides) | Factor-based explanations, human-readable descriptions |
    76тЖТ| ADR-010 | Audit Trail for All Schedule Changes | 12-year government contract requires full attribution and compliance evidence | Implicit logging only | Immutable history tables, user attribution |
    77тЖТ| **ADR-011** | **Unavailability Event Integration** | PM scheduling requires knowledge of trains blocked by corrective work; without this, schedules are invalid on day of execution | Ignore corrective (operationally invalid) | Maximo webhook integration; manual status update UI as fallback |
    78тЖТ| **ADR-012** | **Service Demand as Hard Constraint** | Optimizer must know minimum fleet availability target; cannot validate bow-wave risk without knowing "how many trains do we need?" | Assume 100% availability (unrealistic) | Require service demand input during onboarding; default to 85% fleet availability if unknown |
    79тЖТ| **ADR-013** | **Route-Based Utilization Profiles** | Mileage projection accuracy requires route-specific daily rates, not fleet averages; projection error compounds over 12-year horizon | Fleet-wide average (60,000 km error over 12 years) | Route utilization profiles table; manual override per train; explicit flags for unknown routes |
    80тЖТ| **ADR-014** | **PM History Assumptions for Cold Start** | Data migration must handle incomplete historical records with explicit confidence levels; system must not crash or produce invalid projections from missing data | Assume complete history (crash risk), Ignore missing (false alerts) | Assumption tracking table; inference rules; confidence scoring; manual review queue |
    81тЖТ
    82тЖТ---
    83тЖТ
    84тЖТ## 1.4 Risk Assessment & Mitigation
    85тЖТ
    86тЖТ| Risk ID | Risk Description | Probability | Impact | Mitigation Strategy | Owner |
    87тЖТ|---------|------------------|-------------|--------|---------------------|-------|
    88тЖТ| R-001 | C++ binary build complexity | Medium | High | Containerized build pipeline, pre-built binaries, TypeScript fallback | DevOps |
    89тЖТ| R-002 | Team TypeScript focus with C++ component | Low | Medium | C++ contractor with knowledge transfer, comprehensive integration tests | Tech Lead |
    90тЖТ| R-003 | Strategic/Tactical solver inconsistency | Medium | High | Constraint aggregation layer, cross-validation tests, unified constraint model | Architect |
    91тЖТ| R-004 | Material constraint data quality | High | High | Inventory snapshot validation, fallback to labor-only mode, data quality dashboard | Data Team |
    92тЖТ| R-005 | Operator distrust of optimizer | Medium | High | Explanation traces, gradual rollout, manual override capability, training program | Product |
    93тЖТ| R-006 | Long-term plan instability (jitter) | Medium | Medium | Baseline snapshots, drift thresholds, dampening in projections | Backend |
    94тЖТ| R-007 | deck.gl WebGL performance on low-end devices | Medium | Medium | Progressive enhancement, LOD rendering, tabular fallback | Frontend |
    95тЖТ| R-008 | Data migration quality issues | Medium | High | Parallel-run validation, automated reconciliation, rollback procedures | Data Team |
    96тЖТ| R-009 | Scenario storage scalability | Low | Medium | Event-sourced deltas vs. full clones, automatic scenario expiration | Backend |
    97тЖТ| R-010 | Maximo integration delays | Medium | Medium | CSV interim solution, shadow record system, dedicated integration team | Integration |
    98тЖТ| **R-011** | **No corrective maintenance visibility** | **High** | **Critical** | Unavailability event feed from Maximo; fallback to manual status update UI; alert when train scheduled without availability check | Integration |
    99тЖТ| **R-012** | **Service demand undefined** | **High** | **Critical** | Require service demand input during onboarding; default to 85% fleet availability if unknown; prominent warning in UI when using defaults | Product |
   100тЖТ| **R-013** | **PM history incomplete at migration** | **High** | **High** | Assumption tracking table (`pm_baseline_assumptions`); inference rules from higher-level PMs; confidence scoring; manual review queue for LOW confidence trains | Data Team |
   101тЖТ| **R-014** | **Route utilization unknown** | **Medium** | **High** | Default to fleet-wide average with explicit warning flag; allow manual override per train; route profile editor in admin UI | Product |
   102тЖТ
   103тЖТ---
   104тЖТ
   105тЖТ## 1.5 RFQ Compliance Matrix
   106тЖТ
   107тЖТ| RFQ Requirement | Status | Implementation Section | Notes |
   108тЖТ|-----------------|--------|----------------------|-------|
   109тЖТ| Fleet size (128 trains, expandable to 500+) | ├в┼УтАж | Part 3: Database Schema | UUID-based, no hard limits |
   110тЖТ| Daily mileage ingestion | ├в┼УтАж | Part 4: Ingestion Service | File watcher with validation |
   111тЖТ| Run assignment optimization | ├в┼УтАж | Part 4: Run Assignment Optimizer | C++ CP-SAT with soft constraints |
   112тЖТ| PM scheduling (weekly/monthly) | ├в┼УтАж | Part 4: PM Scheduler | 12-week tactical horizon |
   113тЖТ| Long-term planning (1-10 years) | ├в┼УтАж | Part 4: Strategic Planner | Genetic algorithm with constraint envelope |
   114тЖТ| Man-hour projection & leveling | ├в┼УтАж | Part 4: Man-hour Leveling Engine | Multi-strategy adjustments |
   115тЖТ| Spare parts availability | ├в┼УтАж | Part 3: Material Constraints Schema | Inventory integration, reservation system |
   116тЖТ| Costing module (TCO, variance) | ├в┼УтАж | Part 4: Costing Service | Labor rates, material costs, shop rates |
   117тЖТ| Simulation engine | ├в┼УтАж | Part 4: Simulation Service | Scenario branching, comparison views |
   118тЖТ| Guidance and warnings | ├в┼УтАж | Part 4: Explanation Trace | Factor-based assignment explanations |
   119тЖТ| Maximo integration | ├в┼УтАж | Part 7: Integration | Phased: CSV ├втАатАЩ API ├втАатАЩ Bidirectional |
   120тЖТ| Web-based UI (desktop focus) | ├в┼УтАж | Part 6: User Interface | Infinite Canvas with semantic zoom |
   121тЖТ| Email notifications & reports | ├в┼УтАж | Part 5: API Specification | Notification service, PDF/Excel export |
   122тЖТ| 12-year contract support | ├в┼УтАж | Part 4: Strategic Planner | Baseline/drift architecture |
   123тЖТ| Strategic simulation capability | ├в┼УтАж | Part 4: Simulation Service | What-if scenarios with persistence |
   124тЖТ| Component-level tracking | ├в┼УтАж | Part 3: Component Tracking Schema | Mileage follows components |
   125тЖТ| Data migration | ├в┼УтАж | Part 8: Data Migration | ETL pipeline, validation suite |
   126тЖТ| Documentation deliverables | ├в┼УтАж | Part 8: Documentation Plan | User manual, API reference, training |
   127тЖТ| **Service availability validation** | ├в┼УтАж | Part 3: Service Demand Schema | Min train requirements by day type |
   128тЖТ| **Corrective maintenance awareness** | ├в┼УтАж | Part 7: CM Integration | Maximo unavailability feed |
   129тЖТ
   130тЖТ---
   131тЖТ
   132тЖТ## 1.6 Technology Stack Summary
   133тЖТ
   134тЖТ```
   135тЖТ├втАЭ┼Т├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭ┬Р

   167тЖТ## 1.7 Glossary of Terms
   168тЖТ
   169тЖТ| Term | Definition |
   170тЖТ|------|------------|
   171тЖТ| PM | Preventive Maintenance (C1, C2, C3, C4, Overhaul) |
   172тЖТ| Fixed Parameters | Resource constraints (tracks, man-hours) that bound optimization |
   173тЖТ| Run Assignment | Allocation of a specific train to a specific route for a day |
   174тЖТ| Mileage Leveling | Balancing cumulative mileage across the fleet to equalize wear |
   175тЖТ| Man-hour Leveling | Balancing workforce allocation across time periods |
   176тЖТ| Compliance Window | Acceptable date range for scheduling a PM (earliest to latest) |
   177тЖТ| Bow Wave Risk | Accumulation of deferred maintenance causing future overload |
   178тЖТ| Soft Constraint | Optimization constraint that can be violated with penalty cost |
   179тЖТ| Hard Constraint | Constraint that must never be violated (safety, physical limits) |
   180тЖТ| Strategic Horizon | Long-term planning window (1-10 years, quarterly granularity) |
   181тЖТ| Tactical Horizon | Medium-term planning window (12 weeks, daily granularity) |
   182тЖТ| Baseline | Published snapshot of a plan used as reference for drift analysis |
   183тЖТ| Drift | Deviation between actual state and baseline projection |
   184тЖТ| Scenario | Alternative version of a plan for what-if analysis |
   185тЖТ| Explanation Trace | Human-readable justification for optimizer decisions |
   186тЖТ| TCO | Total Cost of Ownership (12-year projected costs) |
   187тЖТ| Infinite Canvas | Zoomable timeline interface spanning 12 years |
   188тЖТ| Semantic Zoom | Data representation changes based on zoom level |
   189тЖТ| Bill of Materials | List of parts required for a specific PM type |
   190тЖТ| Material Reservation | Soft or hard allocation of inventory to a scheduled PM |
   191тЖТ| **Unavailability Event** | A corrective maintenance event, incident, or external factor that removes a train from service (source: Maximo CM work orders, incidents, or manual entry) |
   192тЖТ| **Service Demand** | Minimum number of trains required for passenger service by day type (weekday, Saturday, Sunday, holiday) |
   193тЖТ| **Utilization Profile** | Expected daily mileage accumulation rate based on route assignment (e.g., NSL = 350 km/day, EWL = 180 km/day) |
   194тЖТ| **Cold Start** | Scenario where PM history is incomplete during data migration, requiring estimation with confidence levels |
   195тЖТ| **Track Capability** | Specific equipment or features available on a maintenance track (e.g., pit access, crane, lifting jacks) |
   196тЖТ| **PM Capability Requirement** | Equipment or track features required to perform a specific PM type |
   197тЖТ
   198тЖТ---
   199тЖТ
   200тЖТ## 1.8 Document Structure
   201тЖТ
   202тЖТThis specification is divided into the following parts:
   203тЖТ
   204тЖТ| Part | Title | Audience |
   205тЖТ|------|-------|----------|
   206тЖТ| 1 | Executive Summary & Decision Record | All stakeholders |
   207тЖТ| 2 | System Architecture | Architects, Tech Leads |
   208тЖТ| 3 | Database Schema | Backend Engineers, DBAs |
   209тЖТ| 4 | Domain Services | Backend Engineers |
   210тЖТ| 5 | API Specification | Frontend Engineers, Integrators |
   211тЖТ| 6 | User Interface Specification | Frontend Engineers, UX Designers |
   212тЖТ| 7 | Integration Specifications | Integration Team |
   213тЖТ| 8 | Operations & Deployment | DevOps, SRE |
   214тЖТ| 9 | Demo Application | All stakeholders |
   215тЖТ
   216тЖТ---
   217тЖТ
   218тЖТ## 1.9 Critical Dependencies
   219тЖТ
   220тЖТThe following external dependencies are **required** for MMS to produce operationally valid schedules:
   221тЖТ
   222тЖТ| Dependency | Source | Frequency | Fallback |
   223тЖТ|------------|--------|-----------|----------|
   224тЖТ| Corrective maintenance status | Maximo webhook | Real-time (or 15-min polling) | Manual status update UI with prominent warning |
   225тЖТ| Inventory positions | ERP/Maximo | Daily snapshot | Assume unlimited (with warning flag) |
   226тЖТ| Service demand | Operations input | Initial + updates | Default to 85% fleet availability |
   227тЖТ| Route utilization profiles | Operations input | Initial + updates | Fleet-wide average (with warning flag) |
   228тЖТ
   229тЖТ---
   230тЖТ
   231тЖТ## 1.10 Change Log
   232тЖТ
   233тЖТ| Version | Date | Author | Changes |
   234тЖТ|---------|------|--------|---------|
   235тЖТ| 1.0 | 2026-01-01 | Initial | Original specification |
   236тЖТ| 2.0 | 2026-01-14 | Review | Added: Material constraints, Explanation trace, Baseline/drift architecture, Scenario management, Component tracking, Audit trail, Constraint aggregation layer |
   237тЖТ| **2.1** | **2026-01-19** | **Gap Analysis** | **Added: ADR-011 through ADR-014 (unavailability, service demand, route utilization, cold start); R-011 through R-014; Scope clarification distinguishing "manages" from "receives"; New glossary terms; Critical dependencies section; Service demand and unavailability awareness in system purpose** |

# Metro Fleet Mileage Management System
# Part 1: Executive Summary & Decision Record

**Document Version:** 2.1  
**Status:** APPROVED FOR DEVELOPMENT  
**Last Updated:** 2026-01-19

---

## 1.1 System Purpose

The Metro Fleet Mileage Management System (MMS) is a constraint-based fleet management platform that optimizes train deployments and maintenance scheduling over a 12-year contract period. It replaces manual Excel-based planning with an integrated system providing:

1. **Daily mileage tracking** with automated ingestion from CSV uploads
2. **Run assignment optimization** balancing mileage across the fleet
3. **Preventive maintenance scheduling** within resource constraints
4. **Strategic planning** for 1-10 year horizons to prevent "bow wave" accumulation
5. **Man-hour leveling** to optimize workforce utilization
6. **Cost forecasting** with TCO projection and variance analysis
7. **Simulation capabilities** for what-if scenario planning
8. **Unified visualization** via an Infinite Canvas interface
9. **Service demand validation** ensuring fleet availability meets operational requirements
10. **Unavailability awareness** integrating corrective maintenance status for schedule validity

---

## 1.2 Project Scope

### 1.2.1 In Scope (MMS Manages)

| Capability | Description |
|------------|-------------|
| Fleet roster management | Trains, cars, components with lifecycle tracking |
| Preventive Maintenance scheduling | C1, C2, C3, C4, Overhaul planning with constraint validation |
| Run assignment optimization | Daily route allocation with mileage balancing |
| Mileage projection and tracking | Route-based utilization profiles, 12-year forecasting |
| Constraint validation | Track capacity, labor, materials, unavailability |
| Maximo integration | Bidirectional sync for PM work orders |
| Multi-year capital planning | Bow-wave detection, cost smoothing |
| Service demand validation | Ensuring minimum fleet availability targets |

### 1.2.2 In Scope (MMS Must Receive/React To)

| External Data | Source | Purpose |
|---------------|--------|---------|
| Corrective maintenance status | Maximo webhook/polling | Block unavailable trains from scheduling |
| Inventory snapshots | ERP/Maximo | Validate material constraints |
| Service demand requirements | Operations team | Set minimum availability targets |

### 1.2.3 Out of Scope (MMS Does Not Execute)

| Capability | Owner System | MMS Interaction |
|------------|--------------|-----------------|
| Corrective maintenance work orders | Maximo CMMS | Receives status updates only |
| Parts inventory management | ERP/Maximo | Consumes snapshots for constraint validation |
| Operator scheduling | Workforce management | None |
| Real-time GPS tracking | OCC systems | None |

> **Critical Clarification:** "Out of Scope" means MMS does not *manage* these functions. However, MMS **must receive** corrective maintenance status to prevent scheduling conflicts. Without this visibility, every PM schedule is potentially invalid on the day of execution.

---

## 1.3 Key Architectural Decisions

| ID | Decision | Rationale | Alternatives Considered | Risk Mitigation |
|----|----------|-----------|------------------------|-----------------|
| ADR-001 | TypeScript-First Architecture | Team expertise, single language for 98% of codebase, strong typing | Java (enterprise), Python (data science) | Node.js fallback path documented |
| ADR-002 | C++ Native Binary for Tactical Optimization | Container density (20-30 pods/node), <5ms cold start, OR-Tools native performance | WASM solver (limited), Pure TS (10x slower) | TypeScript fallback solver, Docker build pipeline |
| ADR-003 | Dual-Horizon Planning (Strategic + Tactical) | RFQ requires 1-10 year planning; single solver cannot handle both granularities | Single solver (bow wave risk), Manual strategic planning | Constraint aggregation layer ensures consistency |
| ADR-004 | Soft Constraints with Explainability | Operations cannot accept "INFEASIBLE"; must provide actionable least-bad solutions | Hard constraints only (operationally unacceptable) | Fallback greedy solver, explanation trace |
| ADR-005 | Material Constraints in Optimizer | PM scheduling without parts availability is operationally useless | Labor-only optimization (cited in reviews as critical gap) | Inventory snapshot integration, reservation system |
| ADR-006 | Baseline/Drift Architecture | Long-term plans cannot recalculate daily; planners need stable reference points | Continuous recalculation (jittery UX, trust erosion) | Published baselines, drift thresholds, dampening |
| ADR-007 | Scenario-Based Simulation | RFQ requires what-if analysis; cannot store in single-reality schema | In-memory only (data loss), Full DB clone (storage bloat) | Event-sourced deltas, materialized comparison views |
| ADR-008 | Infinite Canvas UI (deck.gl) | Semantic zoom from 12-year strategy to daily operations without context switching | Paginated tables (poor UX), Separate views (fragmented) | Progressive enhancement, tabular fallback |
| ADR-009 | Explanation Trace in Optimizer Output | Operators must understand "why" to trust and adopt system recommendations | Black-box output (user rejection, manual overrides) | Factor-based explanations, human-readable descriptions |
| ADR-010 | Audit Trail for All Schedule Changes | 12-year government contract requires full attribution and compliance evidence | Implicit logging only | Immutable history tables, user attribution |
| **ADR-011** | **Unavailability Event Integration** | PM scheduling requires knowledge of trains blocked by corrective work; without this, schedules are invalid on day of execution | Ignore corrective (operationally invalid) | Maximo webhook integration; manual status update UI as fallback |
| **ADR-012** | **Service Demand as Hard Constraint** | Optimizer must know minimum fleet availability target; cannot validate bow-wave risk without knowing "how many trains do we need?" | Assume 100% availability (unrealistic) | Require service demand input during onboarding; default to 85% fleet availability if unknown |
| **ADR-013** | **Route-Based Utilization Profiles** | Mileage projection accuracy requires route-specific daily rates, not fleet averages; projection error compounds over 12-year horizon | Fleet-wide average (60,000 km error over 12 years) | Route utilization profiles table; manual override per train; explicit flags for unknown routes |
| **ADR-014** | **PM History Assumptions for Cold Start** | Data migration must handle incomplete historical records with explicit confidence levels; system must not crash or produce invalid projections from missing data | Assume complete history (crash risk), Ignore missing (false alerts) | Assumption tracking table; inference rules; confidence scoring; manual review queue |

---

## 1.4 Risk Assessment & Mitigation

| Risk ID | Risk Description | Probability | Impact | Mitigation Strategy | Owner |
|---------|------------------|-------------|--------|---------------------|-------|
| R-001 | C++ binary build complexity | Medium | High | Containerized build pipeline, pre-built binaries, TypeScript fallback | DevOps |
| R-002 | Team TypeScript focus with C++ component | Low | Medium | C++ contractor with knowledge transfer, comprehensive integration tests | Tech Lead |
| R-003 | Strategic/Tactical solver inconsistency | Medium | High | Constraint aggregation layer, cross-validation tests, unified constraint model | Architect |
| R-004 | Material constraint data quality | High | High | Inventory snapshot validation, fallback to labor-only mode, data quality dashboard | Data Team |
| R-005 | Operator distrust of optimizer | Medium | High | Explanation traces, gradual rollout, manual override capability, training program | Product |
| R-006 | Long-term plan instability (jitter) | Medium | Medium | Baseline snapshots, drift thresholds, dampening in projections | Backend |
| R-007 | deck.gl WebGL performance on low-end devices | Medium | Medium | Progressive enhancement, LOD rendering, tabular fallback | Frontend |
| R-008 | Data migration quality issues | Medium | High | Parallel-run validation, automated reconciliation, rollback procedures | Data Team |
| R-009 | Scenario storage scalability | Low | Medium | Event-sourced deltas vs. full clones, automatic scenario expiration | Backend |
| R-010 | Maximo integration delays | Medium | Medium | CSV interim solution, shadow record system, dedicated integration team | Integration |
| **R-011** | **No corrective maintenance visibility** | **High** | **Critical** | Unavailability event feed from Maximo; fallback to manual status update UI; alert when train scheduled without availability check | Integration |
| **R-012** | **Service demand undefined** | **High** | **Critical** | Require service demand input during onboarding; default to 85% fleet availability if unknown; prominent warning in UI when using defaults | Product |
| **R-013** | **PM history incomplete at migration** | **High** | **High** | Assumption tracking table (`pm_baseline_assumptions`); inference rules from higher-level PMs; confidence scoring; manual review queue for LOW confidence trains | Data Team |
| **R-014** | **Route utilization unknown** | **Medium** | **High** | Default to fleet-wide average with explicit warning flag; allow manual override per train; route profile editor in admin UI | Product |

---

## 1.5 RFQ Compliance Matrix

| RFQ Requirement | Status | Implementation Section | Notes |
|-----------------|--------|----------------------|-------|
| Fleet size (128 trains, expandable to 500+) | ├в┼УтАж | Part 3: Database Schema | UUID-based, no hard limits |
| Daily mileage ingestion | ├в┼УтАж | Part 4: Ingestion Service | File watcher with validation |
| Run assignment optimization | ├в┼УтАж | Part 4: Run Assignment Optimizer | C++ CP-SAT with soft constraints |
| PM scheduling (weekly/monthly) | ├в┼УтАж | Part 4: PM Scheduler | 12-week tactical horizon |
| Long-term planning (1-10 years) | ├в┼УтАж | Part 4: Strategic Planner | Genetic algorithm with constraint envelope |
| Man-hour projection & leveling | ├в┼УтАж | Part 4: Man-hour Leveling Engine | Multi-strategy adjustments |
| Spare parts availability | ├в┼УтАж | Part 3: Material Constraints Schema | Inventory integration, reservation system |
| Costing module (TCO, variance) | ├в┼УтАж | Part 4: Costing Service | Labor rates, material costs, shop rates |
| Simulation engine | ├в┼УтАж | Part 4: Simulation Service | Scenario branching, comparison views |
| Guidance and warnings | ├в┼УтАж | Part 4: Explanation Trace | Factor-based assignment explanations |
| Maximo integration | ├в┼УтАж | Part 7: Integration | Phased: CSV ├втАатАЩ API ├втАатАЩ Bidirectional |
| Web-based UI (desktop focus) | ├в┼УтАж | Part 6: User Interface | Infinite Canvas with semantic zoom |
| Email notifications & reports | ├в┼УтАж | Part 5: API Specification | Notification service, PDF/Excel export |
| 12-year contract support | ├в┼УтАж | Part 4: Strategic Planner | Baseline/drift architecture |
| Strategic simulation capability | ├в┼УтАж | Part 4: Simulation Service | What-if scenarios with persistence |
| Component-level tracking | ├в┼УтАж | Part 3: Component Tracking Schema | Mileage follows components |
| Data migration | ├в┼УтАж | Part 8: Data Migration | ETL pipeline, validation suite |
| Documentation deliverables | ├в┼УтАж | Part 8: Documentation Plan | User manual, API reference, training |
| **Service availability validation** | ├в┼УтАж | Part 3: Service Demand Schema | Min train requirements by day type |
| **Corrective maintenance awareness** | ├в┼УтАж | Part 7: CM Integration | Maximo unavailability feed |

---

## 1.6 Technology Stack Summary

```
├втАЭ┼Т├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭтВм├втАЭ┬Р

## 1.7 Glossary of Terms

| Term | Definition |
|------|------------|
| PM | Preventive Maintenance (C1, C2, C3, C4, Overhaul) |
| Fixed Parameters | Resource constraints (tracks, man-hours) that bound optimization |
| Run Assignment | Allocation of a specific train to a specific route for a day |
| Mileage Leveling | Balancing cumulative mileage across the fleet to equalize wear |
| Man-hour Leveling | Balancing workforce allocation across time periods |
| Compliance Window | Acceptable date range for scheduling a PM (earliest to latest) |
| Bow Wave Risk | Accumulation of deferred maintenance causing future overload |
| Soft Constraint | Optimization constraint that can be violated with penalty cost |
| Hard Constraint | Constraint that must never be violated (safety, physical limits) |
| Strategic Horizon | Long-term planning window (1-10 years, quarterly granularity) |
| Tactical Horizon | Medium-term planning window (12 weeks, daily granularity) |
| Baseline | Published snapshot of a plan used as reference for drift analysis |
| Drift | Deviation between actual state and baseline projection |
| Scenario | Alternative version of a plan for what-if analysis |
| Explanation Trace | Human-readable justification for optimizer decisions |
| TCO | Total Cost of Ownership (12-year projected costs) |
| Infinite Canvas | Zoomable timeline interface spanning 12 years |
| Semantic Zoom | Data representation changes based on zoom level |
| Bill of Materials | List of parts required for a specific PM type |
| Material Reservation | Soft or hard allocation of inventory to a scheduled PM |
| **Unavailability Event** | A corrective maintenance event, incident, or external factor that removes a train from service (source: Maximo CM work orders, incidents, or manual entry) |
| **Service Demand** | Minimum number of trains required for passenger service by day type (weekday, Saturday, Sunday, holiday) |
| **Utilization Profile** | Expected daily mileage accumulation rate based on route assignment (e.g., NSL = 350 km/day, EWL = 180 km/day) |
| **Cold Start** | Scenario where PM history is incomplete during data migration, requiring estimation with confidence levels |
| **Track Capability** | Specific equipment or features available on a maintenance track (e.g., pit access, crane, lifting jacks) |
| **PM Capability Requirement** | Equipment or track features required to perform a specific PM type |

---

## 1.8 Document Structure

This specification is divided into the following parts:

| Part | Title | Audience |
|------|-------|----------|
| 1 | Executive Summary & Decision Record | All stakeholders |
| 2 | System Architecture | Architects, Tech Leads |
| 3 | Database Schema | Backend Engineers, DBAs |
| 4 | Domain Services | Backend Engineers |
| 5 | API Specification | Frontend Engineers, Integrators |
| 6 | User Interface Specification | Frontend Engineers, UX Designers |
| 7 | Integration Specifications | Integration Team |
| 8 | Operations & Deployment | DevOps, SRE |
| 9 | Demo Application | All stakeholders |

---

## 1.9 Critical Dependencies

The following external dependencies are **required** for MMS to produce operationally valid schedules:

| Dependency | Source | Frequency | Fallback |
|------------|--------|-----------|----------|
| Corrective maintenance status | Maximo webhook | Real-time (or 15-min polling) | Manual status update UI with prominent warning |
| Inventory positions | ERP/Maximo | Daily snapshot | Assume unlimited (with warning flag) |
| Service demand | Operations input | Initial + updates | Default to 85% fleet availability |
| Route utilization profiles | Operations input | Initial + updates | Fleet-wide average (with warning flag) |

---

## 1.10 Change Log

| Version | Date | Author | Changes |
|---------|------|--------|---------|
| 1.0 | 2026-01-01 | Initial | Original specification |
| 2.0 | 2026-01-14 | Review | Added: Material constraints, Explanation trace, Baseline/drift architecture, Scenario management, Component tracking, Audit trail, Constraint aggregation layer |
| **2.1** | **2026-01-19** | **Gap Analysis** | **Added: ADR-011 through ADR-014 (unavailability, service demand, route utilization, cold start); R-011 through R-014; Scope clarification distinguishing "manages" from "receives"; New glossary terms; Critical dependencies section; Service demand and unavailability awareness in system purpose** |

    36тЖТ## 3.2 Core Fleet Tables
    37тЖТ
    38тЖТ```sql
    39тЖТ-- Enable required extensions

    42тЖТ

    57тЖТCREATE TYPE unavailability_status AS ENUM ('ACTIVE', 'RESOLVED');
    58тЖТCREATE TYPE track_capability AS ENUM ('PIT', 'CRANE', 'LIFTING_JACKS', 'WHEEL_LATHE', 'CATENARY_ISOLATION', 'ROOF_ACCESS', 'WASH', 'DROP_TABLE');
    59тЖТCREATE TYPE constraint_type AS ENUM ('SIGNALING', 'GAUGE', 'POWER_SUPPLY', 'PLATFORM_LENGTH', 'MAX_SPEED', 'TUNNEL_CLEARANCE');
    60тЖТCREATE TYPE assumption_type AS ENUM ('INFERRED_FROM_COMMISSIONING', 'INFERRED_FROM_HIGHER_PM', 'MANUAL_OVERRIDE', 'IMPORTED', 'NO_HISTORY');
    61тЖТCREATE TYPE confidence_level AS ENUM ('HIGH', 'MEDIUM', 'LOW', 'ESTIMATED');
    62тЖТ
    63тЖТ-- Depots

    74тЖТ

    76тЖТCREATE TABLE fleets (

    96тЖТ    fitness_status fitness_status DEFAULT 'FIT',
    97тЖТ    fitness_notes TEXT,
    98тЖТ    current_depot_id UUID REFERENCES depots(id),
    99тЖТ    home_depot_id UUID REFERENCES depots(id),
   100тЖТ    active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
   101тЖТ    
   102тЖТ    -- For mileage balancing
   103тЖТ    target_mileage_km INTEGER,  -- Calculated target for fleet balance
   104тЖТ    mileage_variance_km INTEGER GENERATED ALWAYS AS 
   105тЖТ        (current_mileage_km - COALESCE(target_mileage_km, current_mileage_km)) STORED,
   106тЖТ    
   107тЖТ    -- Canvas display properties

   114тЖТ
   115тЖТCREATE INDEX idx_trains_fleet ON trains(fleet_id);
   116тЖТCREATE INDEX idx_trains_fitness ON trains(fitness_status) WHERE active = TRUE;
   117тЖТCREATE INDEX idx_trains_depot ON trains(current_depot_id);
   118тЖТ
   119тЖТ-- Routes
   120тЖТCREATE TABLE routes (
   121тЖТ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),

   130тЖТ    signaling_system VARCHAR(30),                    -- 'ATC', 'CBTC', 'ERTMS', 'LEGACY'

   132тЖТ    tunnel_clearance_mm INTEGER,                     -- Maximum vehicle width allowed

   135тЖТ

   147тЖТ-- Tracks (maintenance bays)

   156тЖТ

   163тЖТCREATE INDEX idx_tracks_depot_type ON tracks(depot_id, track_type) WHERE active = TRUE;

   165тЖТ
   166тЖТ---
   167тЖТ
   168тЖТ## 3.2.1 Service Demand Tables (NEW v2.1)
   169тЖТ
   170тЖТService demand defines the minimum number of trains required for passenger operations by day type. This is a **hard constraint** for the optimizer - it cannot schedule maintenance that would violate availability targets.
   171тЖТ
   172тЖТ```sql
   173тЖТ-- Service demand by fleet and day type
   174тЖТCREATE TABLE service_demand (
   175тЖТ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   176тЖТ    fleet_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES fleets(id),
   177тЖТ    effective_date DATE NOT NULL,
   178тЖТ    day_type day_type NOT NULL,  -- 'WEEKDAY', 'SATURDAY', 'SUNDAY', 'HOLIDAY'
   179тЖТ    
   180тЖТ    -- Minimum availability requirements
   181тЖТ    min_trains_required INTEGER NOT NULL,
   182тЖТ    peak_am_requirement INTEGER,       -- 6:00-9:00 requirement
   183тЖТ    midday_requirement INTEGER,        -- 9:00-16:00 requirement  
   184тЖТ    peak_pm_requirement INTEGER,       -- 16:00-19:00 requirement
   185тЖТ    evening_requirement INTEGER,       -- 19:00-23:00 requirement
   186тЖТ    standby_buffer INTEGER DEFAULT 2,  -- Additional trains for contingency
   187тЖТ    
   188тЖТ    -- Metadata
   189тЖТ    source VARCHAR(50),                -- 'OPERATIONS', 'CONTRACT', 'MANUAL'
   190тЖТ    notes TEXT,
   191тЖТ    
   192тЖТ    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   193тЖТ    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   194тЖТ    created_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
   195тЖТ    
   196тЖТ    UNIQUE(fleet_id, effective_date, day_type)
   197тЖТ);
   198тЖТ
   199тЖТCREATE INDEX idx_service_demand_fleet ON service_demand(fleet_id, effective_date DESC);
   200тЖТCREATE INDEX idx_service_demand_lookup ON service_demand(fleet_id, day_type, effective_date DESC);
   201тЖТ
   202тЖТ-- Example data:
   203тЖТ-- INSERT INTO service_demand (fleet_id, effective_date, day_type, min_trains_required, peak_am_requirement, standby_buffer)
   204тЖТ-- VALUES ('fleet-ks-uuid', '2026-01-01', 'WEEKDAY', 45, 52, 3);
   205тЖТ-- VALUES ('fleet-ks-uuid', '2026-01-01', 'SATURDAY', 32, 35, 2);
   206тЖТ-- VALUES ('fleet-ks-uuid', '2026-01-01', 'SUNDAY', 28, 30, 2);
   207тЖТ```
   208тЖТ
   209тЖТ---
   210тЖТ
   211тЖТ## 3.2.2 Route Utilization Profiles (NEW v2.1)
   212тЖТ
   213тЖТRoute utilization profiles capture the expected daily mileage by route, enabling accurate long-term projections. Without this, mileage projection uses fleet-wide averages which can be off by 60,000+ km over 12 years.
   214тЖТ
   215тЖТ```sql
   216тЖТ-- Route-specific utilization profiles
   217тЖТCREATE TABLE route_utilization_profiles (
   218тЖТ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   219тЖТ    route_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES routes(id),
   220тЖТ    effective_date DATE NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_DATE,
   221тЖТ    
   222тЖТ    -- Daily mileage by day type (km)
   223тЖТ    weekday_km INTEGER NOT NULL,
   224тЖТ    saturday_km INTEGER,
   225тЖТ    sunday_km INTEGER,
   226тЖТ    holiday_km INTEGER,
   227тЖТ    
   228тЖТ    -- Seasonal adjustments
   229тЖТ    peak_season_multiplier DECIMAL(3,2) DEFAULT 1.0,  -- e.g., 1.15 for CNY
   230тЖТ    off_peak_multiplier DECIMAL(3,2) DEFAULT 1.0,
   231тЖТ    
   232тЖТ    -- Service characteristics
   233тЖТ    service_hours_per_day DECIMAL(4,1),               -- Operating hours
   234тЖТ    round_trip_km DECIMAL(6,2),                       -- Single round trip distance
   235тЖТ    trips_per_day INTEGER,                            -- Expected service runs
   236тЖТ    
   237тЖТ    -- Metadata
   238тЖТ    source VARCHAR(50),                               -- 'MEASURED', 'ESTIMATED', 'CONTRACT'
   239тЖТ    confidence confidence_level DEFAULT 'MEDIUM',
   240тЖТ    notes TEXT,
   241тЖТ    
   242тЖТ    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   243тЖТ    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   244тЖТ    
   245тЖТ    UNIQUE(route_id, effective_date)
   246тЖТ);
   247тЖТ
   248тЖТCREATE INDEX idx_route_util_route ON route_utilization_profiles(route_id, effective_date DESC);
   249тЖТ
   250тЖТ-- Example data:
   251тЖТ-- North-South Line: High utilization (350 km/day weekday)
   252тЖТ-- INSERT INTO route_utilization_profiles (route_id, effective_date, weekday_km, saturday_km, sunday_km, confidence)
   253тЖТ-- VALUES ('route-nsl-uuid', '2026-01-01', 350, 280, 240, 'HIGH');
   254тЖТ-- East-West Line: Medium utilization (220 km/day weekday)
   255тЖТ-- VALUES ('route-ewl-uuid', '2026-01-01', 220, 180, 150, 'HIGH');
   256тЖТ```
   257тЖТ
   258тЖТ---
   259тЖТ
   260тЖТ## 3.2.3 Unavailability Events (NEW v2.1)
   261тЖТ
   262тЖТUnavailability events track trains that cannot be scheduled due to corrective maintenance, incidents, or other blocking factors. This is a **critical integration point** with Maximo CMMS.
   263тЖТ
   264тЖТ```sql
   265тЖТ-- Unavailability events (corrective maintenance, incidents, etc.)
   266тЖТCREATE TABLE unavailability_events (
   267тЖТ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   268тЖТ    train_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES trains(id),
   269тЖТ    
   270тЖТ    -- Event classification
   271тЖТ    event_type unavailability_type NOT NULL,  -- 'CORRECTIVE', 'INCIDENT', etc.
   272тЖТ    status unavailability_status NOT NULL DEFAULT 'ACTIVE',
   273тЖТ    restriction_level fitness_status NOT NULL, -- Uses existing ENUM: 'RESTRICTED' or 'GROUNDED'
   274тЖТ    
   275тЖТ    -- Timing
   276тЖТ    reported_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
   277тЖТ    estimated_repair_hours INTEGER,
   278тЖТ    estimated_return_date DATE,
   279тЖТ    actual_return_date DATE,
   280тЖТ    
   281тЖТ    -- External system linkage
   282тЖТ    source_system VARCHAR(30),        -- 'MAXIMO', 'OCC', 'MANUAL'
   283тЖТ    external_reference VARCHAR(50),   -- Maximo WO number (e.g., 'WO-2026-001234')
   284тЖТ    external_status VARCHAR(30),      -- Maximo status (e.g., 'INPRG', 'WAPPR')
   285тЖТ    
   286тЖТ    -- Description
   287тЖТ    description TEXT,
   288тЖТ    failure_code VARCHAR(30),         -- Standard failure classification
   289тЖТ    affected_system VARCHAR(50),      -- 'TRACTION', 'BRAKES', 'DOORS', 'HVAC', etc.
   290тЖТ    
   291тЖТ    -- Scheduling impact flags
   292тЖТ    blocks_pm_scheduling BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
   293тЖТ    blocks_run_assignment BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
   294тЖТ    
   295тЖТ    -- Resolution
   296тЖТ    resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   297тЖТ    resolved_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
   298тЖТ    resolution_notes TEXT,
   299тЖТ    
   300тЖТ    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   301тЖТ    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   302тЖТ    
   303тЖТ    -- Constraint: Cannot have resolved data if status is ACTIVE
   304тЖТ    CONSTRAINT chk_resolved_status CHECK (
   305тЖТ        (status = 'ACTIVE' AND resolved_at IS NULL) OR
   306тЖТ        (status = 'RESOLVED' AND resolved_at IS NOT NULL)
   307тЖТ    )
   308тЖТ);
   309тЖТ
   310тЖТ-- Primary query: Find active unavailability for a train
   311тЖТCREATE INDEX idx_unavailability_active ON unavailability_events(train_id, status) 
   312тЖТ    WHERE status = 'ACTIVE';
   313тЖТ-- Dashboard query: All active unavailability by estimated return
   314тЖТCREATE INDEX idx_unavailability_return ON unavailability_events(estimated_return_date) 
   315тЖТ    WHERE status = 'ACTIVE';
   316тЖТ-- Maximo sync: Find by external reference
   317тЖТCREATE INDEX idx_unavailability_external ON unavailability_events(source_system, external_reference);
   318тЖТ-- Audit: Find by type and date range
   319тЖТCREATE INDEX idx_unavailability_audit ON unavailability_events(event_type, reported_at DESC);
   320тЖТ
   321тЖТ-- Trigger to update train fitness_status when unavailability changes
   322тЖТCREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION sync_train_fitness_from_unavailability()
   323тЖТRETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
   324тЖТBEGIN
   325тЖТ    IF TG_OP = 'INSERT' OR TG_OP = 'UPDATE' THEN
   326тЖТ        IF NEW.status = 'ACTIVE' THEN
   327тЖТ            UPDATE trains 
   328тЖТ            SET fitness_status = NEW.restriction_level,
   329тЖТ                fitness_notes = COALESCE(NEW.description, 'Unavailability event: ' || NEW.event_type),
   330тЖТ                updated_at = NOW()
   331тЖТ            WHERE id = NEW.train_id;
   332тЖТ        ELSIF NEW.status = 'RESOLVED' THEN
   333тЖТ            -- Only reset to FIT if no other active unavailability exists
   334тЖТ            IF NOT EXISTS (
   335тЖТ                SELECT 1 FROM unavailability_events 
   336тЖТ                WHERE train_id = NEW.train_id 
   337тЖТ                AND status = 'ACTIVE' 
   338тЖТ                AND id != NEW.id
   339тЖТ            ) THEN
   340тЖТ                UPDATE trains 
   341тЖТ                SET fitness_status = 'FIT',
   342тЖТ                    fitness_notes = NULL,
   343тЖТ                    updated_at = NOW()
   344тЖТ                WHERE id = NEW.train_id;
   345тЖТ            END IF;
   346тЖТ        END IF;
   347тЖТ    END IF;
   348тЖТ    RETURN NEW;
   349тЖТEND;
   350тЖТ$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
   351тЖТ
   352тЖТCREATE TRIGGER trg_sync_train_fitness
   353тЖТAFTER INSERT OR UPDATE ON unavailability_events
   354тЖТFOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION sync_train_fitness_from_unavailability();
   355тЖТ```
   356тЖТ
   357тЖТ---
   358тЖТ
   359тЖТ## 3.2.4 Train-Route Compatibility (NEW v2.1)
   360тЖТ
   361тЖТDefines which trains can operate on which routes based on technical constraints (signaling, gauge, power supply, etc.). Prevents the optimizer from assigning trains to incompatible routes.
   362тЖТ
   363тЖТ```sql
   364тЖТ-- Train-route compatibility matrix
   365тЖТCREATE TABLE train_route_compatibility (
   366тЖТ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   367тЖТ    
   368тЖТ    -- Can be at train or fleet level
   369тЖТ    train_id UUID REFERENCES trains(id),
   370тЖТ    fleet_id UUID REFERENCES fleets(id),
   371тЖТ    route_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES routes(id),
   372тЖТ    
   373тЖТ    -- Compatibility determination
   374тЖТ    is_compatible BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE,
   375тЖТ    constraint_type constraint_type,   -- 'SIGNALING', 'GAUGE', 'POWER_SUPPLY', etc.
   376тЖТ    restriction_reason TEXT,           -- Human-readable explanation
   377тЖТ    
   378тЖТ    -- Validity period (for temporary restrictions)
   379тЖТ    effective_date DATE DEFAULT CURRENT_DATE,
   380тЖТ    expiry_date DATE,                  -- NULL = permanent
   381тЖТ    
   382тЖТ    -- Metadata
   383тЖТ    verified_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
   384тЖТ    verified_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   385тЖТ    notes TEXT,
   386тЖТ    
   387тЖТ    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   388тЖТ    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   389тЖТ    
   390тЖТ    -- Must specify either train_id OR fleet_id, not both
   391тЖТ    CONSTRAINT chk_train_or_fleet CHECK (
   392тЖТ        (train_id IS NOT NULL AND fleet_id IS NULL) OR
   393тЖТ        (train_id IS NULL AND fleet_id IS NOT NULL)
   394тЖТ    )
   395тЖТ);
   396тЖТ
   397тЖТCREATE INDEX idx_compatibility_route ON train_route_compatibility(route_id, is_compatible);
   398тЖТCREATE INDEX idx_compatibility_train ON train_route_compatibility(train_id, is_compatible) 
   399тЖТ    WHERE train_id IS NOT NULL;
   400тЖТCREATE INDEX idx_compatibility_fleet ON train_route_compatibility(fleet_id, is_compatible) 
   401тЖТ    WHERE fleet_id IS NOT NULL;
   402тЖТCREATE INDEX idx_compatibility_active ON train_route_compatibility(effective_date, expiry_date)
   403тЖТ    WHERE is_compatible = FALSE;
   404тЖТ
   405тЖТ-- Example data:
   406тЖТ-- Fleet KS cannot run on Line 4 (no CBTC equipment)
   407тЖТ-- INSERT INTO train_route_compatibility (fleet_id, route_id, is_compatible, constraint_type, restriction_reason)
   408тЖТ-- VALUES ('fleet-ks-uuid', 'route-line4-uuid', FALSE, 'SIGNALING', 'KS fleet lacks CBTC - only ATC equipped');
   409тЖТ
   410тЖТ-- Train 1042 temporarily restricted from NEL (oversized pantograph)
   411тЖТ-- INSERT INTO train_route_compatibility (train_id, route_id, is_compatible, constraint_type, restriction_reason, effective_date, expiry_date)
   412тЖТ-- VALUES ('train-1042-uuid', 'route-nel-uuid', FALSE, 'TUNNEL_CLEARANCE', 'Temporary - pantograph modification pending', '2026-01-01', '2026-03-31');
   413тЖТ```

   416тЖТ
   417тЖТ## 3.2.5 Track Capabilities (NEW v2.1)
   418тЖТ
   419тЖТDefines the specific equipment and features available on each maintenance track. Enables constraint-based scheduling that matches PM requirements to track capabilities.
   420тЖТ
   421тЖТ```sql
   422тЖТ-- Track capability definitions
   423тЖТCREATE TABLE track_capabilities (
   424тЖТ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   425тЖТ    track_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES tracks(id),
   426тЖТ    capability track_capability NOT NULL,
   427тЖТ    -- 'PIT', 'CRANE', 'LIFTING_JACKS', 'WHEEL_LATHE', 
   428тЖТ    -- 'CATENARY_ISOLATION', 'ROOF_ACCESS', 'WASH', 'DROP_TABLE'
   429тЖТ    
   430тЖТ    -- Capacity limits for this capability
   431тЖТ    capacity_limit JSONB,  -- e.g., {"max_axle_load_tonnes": 17, "max_lift_tonnes": 25}
   432тЖТ    
   433тЖТ    -- Availability
   434тЖТ    is_operational BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
   435тЖТ    maintenance_schedule TEXT,         -- e.g., "Unavailable Tuesdays 06:00-10:00"
   436тЖТ    
   437тЖТ    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   438тЖТ    
   439тЖТ    UNIQUE(track_id, capability)
   440тЖТ);
   441тЖТ
   442тЖТCREATE INDEX idx_track_cap_track ON track_capabilities(track_id);
   443тЖТCREATE INDEX idx_track_cap_type ON track_capabilities(capability) WHERE is_operational = TRUE;
   444тЖТ
   445тЖТ-- PM type capability requirements
   446тЖТCREATE TABLE pm_capability_requirements (
   447тЖТ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   448тЖТ    pm_type_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES pm_types(id),
   449тЖТ    capability track_capability NOT NULL,
   450тЖТ    is_mandatory BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,  -- FALSE = preferred but not required
   451тЖТ    
   452тЖТ    notes TEXT,                         -- Explanation of why this capability is needed
   453тЖТ    
   454тЖТ    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   455тЖТ    
   456тЖТ    UNIQUE(pm_type_id, capability)
   457тЖТ);
   458тЖТ
   459тЖТCREATE INDEX idx_pm_cap_req_pm ON pm_capability_requirements(pm_type_id);
   460тЖТCREATE INDEX idx_pm_cap_req_cap ON pm_capability_requirements(capability);
   461тЖТ
   462тЖТ-- Example data:
   463тЖТ-- Track T1 at Bishan has pit, crane, and lifting jacks
   464тЖТ-- INSERT INTO track_capabilities (track_id, capability)
   465тЖТ-- VALUES ('track-t1-bishan-uuid', 'PIT'),
   466тЖТ--        ('track-t1-bishan-uuid', 'CRANE'),
   467тЖТ--        ('track-t1-bishan-uuid', 'LIFTING_JACKS');
   468тЖТ
   469тЖТ-- C4 overhaul requires pit and lifting jacks (mandatory), crane (preferred)
   470тЖТ-- INSERT INTO pm_capability_requirements (pm_type_id, capability, is_mandatory)
   471тЖТ-- VALUES ('pm-c4-uuid', 'PIT', TRUE),
   472тЖТ--        ('pm-c4-uuid', 'LIFTING_JACKS', TRUE),
   473тЖТ--        ('pm-c4-uuid', 'CRANE', FALSE);
   474тЖТ```
   475тЖТ

   477тЖТ
   478тЖТ## 3.2.6 PM Baseline Assumptions (NEW v2.1)
   479тЖТ
   480тЖТHandles cold start scenarios where PM history is incomplete during data migration. Tracks assumptions with confidence levels and flags records requiring manual review.
   481тЖТ
   482тЖТ```sql
   483тЖТ-- PM history baseline assumptions for cold start scenarios
   484тЖТCREATE TABLE pm_baseline_assumptions (
   485тЖТ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   486тЖТ    train_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES trains(id),
   487тЖТ    pm_type_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES pm_types(id),
   488тЖТ    
   489тЖТ    -- Assumption details
   490тЖТ    assumption_type assumption_type NOT NULL,
   491тЖТ    -- 'INFERRED_FROM_COMMISSIONING' - assume first PM at commissioning date + interval
   492тЖТ    -- 'INFERRED_FROM_HIGHER_PM' - derived from C4/OH completion implies C1/C2/C3 done
   493тЖТ    -- 'MANUAL_OVERRIDE' - operator entered value
   494тЖТ    -- 'IMPORTED' - from legacy system with known quality
   495тЖТ    -- 'NO_HISTORY' - no data available, using conservative estimate
   496тЖТ    
   497тЖТ    -- Assumed baseline values
   498тЖТ    assumed_last_pm_date DATE,
   499тЖТ    assumed_last_pm_mileage INTEGER,
   500тЖТ    confidence_level confidence_level NOT NULL,  -- 'HIGH', 'MEDIUM', 'LOW', 'ESTIMATED'
   501тЖТ    
   502тЖТ    -- Inference source documentation
   503тЖТ    inference_source TEXT,             -- Explanation of how assumption was derived
   504тЖТ    inference_data JSONB,              -- Supporting data (e.g., higher PM dates)
   505тЖТ    
   506тЖТ    -- Review workflow
   507тЖТ    requires_review BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
   508тЖТ    reviewed_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
   509тЖТ    reviewed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   510тЖТ    review_notes TEXT,
   511тЖТ    
   512тЖТ    -- When applied
   513тЖТ    applied_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   514тЖТ    applied_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
   515тЖТ    
   516тЖТ    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   517тЖТ    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   518тЖТ    
   519тЖТ    UNIQUE(train_id, pm_type_id)
   520тЖТ);
   521тЖТ
   522тЖТCREATE INDEX idx_assumptions_train ON pm_baseline_assumptions(train_id);
   523тЖТCREATE INDEX idx_assumptions_review ON pm_baseline_assumptions(requires_review) 
   524тЖТ    WHERE requires_review = TRUE;
   525тЖТCREATE INDEX idx_assumptions_confidence ON pm_baseline_assumptions(confidence_level)
   526тЖТ    WHERE confidence_level IN ('LOW', 'ESTIMATED');
   527тЖТ
   528тЖТ-- Example inference rules (implemented in application logic):
   529тЖТ-- 1. If train commissioned 2020-01-01 and C1 interval is 6 months:
   530тЖТ--    assumed_last_pm_date = 2020-01-01, confidence = 'LOW'
   531тЖТ-- 2. If C4 completed 2024-06-15 and C4 satisfies C1/C2/C3:
   532тЖТ--    For C1/C2/C3: assumed_last_pm_date = 2024-06-15, confidence = 'HIGH'
   533тЖТ-- 3. If no data at all:
   534тЖТ--    assumed_last_pm_date = commissioning_date, confidence = 'ESTIMATED', requires_review = TRUE
   535тЖТ```
   536тЖТ

   538тЖТ
   539тЖТ## 3.3 Mileage Tracking Tables
   540тЖТ
   541тЖТ```sql
   542тЖТ-- Meter events (odometer replacements, resets, swaps)
   543тЖТCREATE TABLE meter_events (
   544тЖТ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   545тЖТ    train_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES trains(id),
   546тЖТ    event_date DATE NOT NULL,
   547тЖТ    event_type VARCHAR(30) NOT NULL,  -- 'REPLACEMENT', 'RESET', 'CALIBRATION', 'SWAP'
   548тЖТ    
   549тЖТ    old_meter_serial VARCHAR(50),
   550тЖТ    new_meter_serial VARCHAR(50),
   551тЖТ    old_meter_reading INTEGER,
   552тЖТ    new_meter_reading INTEGER,
   553тЖТ    adjustment_km INTEGER,  -- Cumulative mileage adjustment to apply
   554тЖТ    
   555тЖТ    reason TEXT,
   556тЖТ    approved_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
   557тЖТ    approved_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   558тЖТ    
   559тЖТ    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   560тЖТ    created_by UUID REFERENCES users(id)
   561тЖТ);
   562тЖТ
   563тЖТCREATE INDEX idx_meter_events_train ON meter_events(train_id, event_date DESC);
   564тЖТ
   565тЖТ-- Daily mileage readings (TimescaleDB hypertable)
   566тЖТCREATE TABLE mileage_readings (
   567тЖТ    train_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES trains(id),
   568тЖТ    reading_date DATE NOT NULL,
   569тЖТ    
   570тЖТ    -- Raw and adjusted values
   571тЖТ    raw_reading INTEGER NOT NULL,        -- Odometer value from CSV
   572тЖТ    adjusted_reading INTEGER NOT NULL,   -- After meter event adjustments
   573тЖТ    daily_km INTEGER,                    -- Calculated: today - yesterday
   574тЖТ    
   575тЖТ    -- Validation
   576тЖТ    validation_status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'PENDING',  -- 'PENDING', 'VALID', 'ANOMALY', 'CORRECTED'
   577тЖТ    anomaly_type VARCHAR(30),            -- 'NEGATIVE', 'SPIKE', 'MISSING', 'DUPLICATE'
   578тЖТ    anomaly_notes TEXT,
   579тЖТ    
   580тЖТ    -- Source tracking
   581тЖТ    source_file VARCHAR(255),
   582тЖТ    meter_event_id UUID REFERENCES meter_events(id),
   583тЖТ    
   584тЖТ    ingested_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   585тЖТ    
   586тЖТ    PRIMARY KEY (train_id, reading_date)
   587тЖТ);
   588тЖТ
   589тЖТ-- Convert to TimescaleDB hypertable
   590тЖТSELECT create_hypertable('mileage_readings', 'reading_date', 
   591тЖТ    chunk_time_interval => INTERVAL '7 days',
   592тЖТ    if_not_exists => TRUE
   593тЖТ);
   594тЖТ
   595тЖТ-- Compression policy (compress data older than 30 days)
   596тЖТSELECT add_compression_policy('mileage_readings', INTERVAL '30 days');
   597тЖТ
   598тЖТ-- Retention policy (keep 13 years for contract + buffer)
   599тЖТSELECT add_retention_policy('mileage_readings', INTERVAL '13 years');
   600тЖТ
   601тЖТ-- Indexes for common queries
   602тЖТCREATE INDEX idx_mileage_train_date ON mileage_readings (train_id, reading_date DESC);
   603тЖТCREATE INDEX idx_mileage_validation ON mileage_readings (validation_status, reading_date DESC) 
   604тЖТ    WHERE validation_status != 'VALID';
   605тЖТ
   606тЖТ-- Continuous aggregate for dashboard queries
   607тЖТCREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW daily_fleet_mileage
   608тЖТWITH (timescaledb.continuous) AS
   609тЖТSELECT 
   610тЖТ    time_bucket('1 day', reading_date) AS bucket,
   611тЖТ    t.fleet_id,
   612тЖТ    COUNT(*) as train_count,
   613тЖТ    SUM(daily_km) as total_daily_km,
   614тЖТ    AVG(daily_km) as avg_daily_km,
   615тЖТ    MIN(daily_km) as min_daily_km,
   616тЖТ    MAX(daily_km) as max_daily_km,
   617тЖТ    STDDEV(daily_km) as stddev_daily_km
   618тЖТFROM mileage_readings mr
   619тЖТJOIN trains t ON mr.train_id = t.id
   620тЖТWHERE mr.validation_status = 'VALID'
   621тЖТGROUP BY bucket, t.fleet_id
   622тЖТWITH NO DATA;
   623тЖТ
   624тЖТSELECT add_continuous_aggregate_policy('daily_fleet_mileage',
   625тЖТ    start_offset => INTERVAL '3 days',
   626тЖТ    end_offset => INTERVAL '1 hour',
   627тЖТ    schedule_interval => INTERVAL '1 hour'
   628тЖТ);
   629тЖТ```
   630тЖТ

   633тЖТ## 3.4 Component Tracking Tables
   634тЖТ
   635тЖТ```sql
   636тЖТ-- Train components (bogies, motors, pantographs, etc.)
   637тЖТCREATE TABLE train_components (
   638тЖТ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   639тЖТ    component_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,  -- 'BOGIE', 'TRACTION_MOTOR', 'PANTOGRAPH', 'BRAKE_UNIT'
   640тЖТ    serial_number VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
   641тЖТ    manufacturer VARCHAR(100),
   642тЖТ    model VARCHAR(100),
   643тЖТ    
   644тЖТ    -- Current installation
   645тЖТ    current_train_id UUID REFERENCES trains(id),
   646тЖТ    current_position VARCHAR(50),  -- e.g., 'CAR_1_BOGIE_A', 'CAR_3_MOTOR_L'
   647тЖТ    installed_date DATE,
   648тЖТ    installed_mileage_km INTEGER,  -- Train mileage at installation
   649тЖТ    
   650тЖТ    -- Component lifecycle
   651тЖТ    commissioning_date DATE,
   652тЖТ    component_mileage_km INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,  -- Cumulative mileage on this component
   653тЖТ    last_overhaul_date DATE,
   654тЖТ    last_overhaul_mileage_km INTEGER,
   655тЖТ    
   656тЖТ    -- Status
   657тЖТ    status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'IN_SERVICE',  -- 'IN_SERVICE', 'SPARE', 'IN_OVERHAUL', 'CONDEMNED'
   658тЖТ    condition_score INTEGER,  -- 1-100 condition rating
   659тЖТ    
   660тЖТ    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   661тЖТ    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
   662тЖТ);
   663тЖТ
   664тЖТCREATE INDEX idx_components_train ON train_components(current_train_id) WHERE status = 'IN_SERVICE';
   665тЖТCREATE INDEX idx_components_type ON train_components(component_type, status);
   666тЖТ
   667тЖТ-- Component installation history
   668тЖТCREATE TABLE component_installations (
   669тЖТ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   670тЖТ    component_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES train_components(id),
   671тЖТ    train_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES trains(id),
   672тЖТ    position VARCHAR(50),
   673тЖТ    
   674тЖТ    installed_date DATE NOT NULL,
   675тЖТ    installed_train_mileage_km INTEGER NOT NULL,
   676тЖТ    installed_component_mileage_km INTEGER NOT NULL,
   677тЖТ    
   678тЖТ    removed_date DATE,
   679тЖТ    removed_train_mileage_km INTEGER,
   680тЖТ    removed_component_mileage_km INTEGER,
   681тЖТ    
   682тЖТ    removal_reason VARCHAR(50),  -- 'SCHEDULED_OVERHAUL', 'FAILURE', 'SWAP', 'UPGRADE'
   683тЖТ    work_order_id VARCHAR(50),   -- Link to Maximo
   684тЖТ    
   685тЖТ    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
   686тЖТ);
   687тЖТ
   688тЖТCREATE INDEX idx_installations_component ON component_installations(component_id, installed_date DESC);
   689тЖТCREATE INDEX idx_installations_train ON component_installations(train_id, installed_date DESC);
   690тЖТ
   691тЖТ-- Component-based mileage adjustments
   692тЖТ-- When a component is swapped, PM thresholds follow the component, not the train
   693тЖТCREATE TABLE component_pm_tracking (
   694тЖТ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   695тЖТ    component_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES train_components(id),
   696тЖТ    pm_type_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES pm_types(id),
   697тЖТ    
   698тЖТ    last_pm_date DATE,
   699тЖТ    last_pm_mileage_km INTEGER,
   700тЖТ    next_pm_due_mileage_km INTEGER,
   701тЖТ    next_pm_due_date DATE,
   702тЖТ    
   703тЖТ    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   704тЖТ    
   705тЖТ    UNIQUE(component_id, pm_type_id)
   706тЖТ);
   707тЖТ```
   708тЖТ
   709тЖТ---
   710тЖТ
   711тЖТ## 3.5 PM Management Tables
   712тЖТ
   713тЖТ```sql
   714тЖТ-- PM types (maintenance task definitions)
   715тЖТCREATE TABLE pm_types (
   716тЖТ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   717тЖТ    fleet_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES fleets(id),
   718тЖТ    pm_code VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,      -- 'C1', 'C2', 'C3', 'C4', 'OVERHAUL'
   719тЖТ    pm_name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
   720тЖТ    version INTEGER DEFAULT 1,
   721тЖТ    
   722тЖТ    -- Interval thresholds (whichever comes first)
   723тЖТ    interval_km INTEGER,               -- Mileage-based trigger
   724тЖТ    interval_months INTEGER,           -- Time-based trigger (industry standard)
   725тЖТ    
   726тЖТ    -- PM Hierarchy: which lower-level PMs does this PM satisfy?
   727тЖТ    -- When a C4 is completed, it satisfies C1/C2/C3 requirements
   728тЖТ    -- This resets their mileage counters from the C4 completion point
   729тЖТ    -- Example: C4 row has satisfies_codes = ['C1', 'C2', 'C3']
   730тЖТ    satisfies_codes VARCHAR(20)[],
   731тЖТ    
   732тЖТ    -- Resource requirements
   733тЖТ    estimated_hours DECIMAL(6,2) NOT NULL,
   734тЖТ    track_type track_type NOT NULL,
   735тЖТ    min_track_days INTEGER DEFAULT 1,  -- How many days track is occupied
   736тЖТ    
   737тЖТ    -- Compliance window (tolerance for scheduling)
   738тЖТ    early_tolerance_pct DECIMAL(5,2) DEFAULT 10.0,   -- Can do 10% early
   739тЖТ    late_tolerance_pct DECIMAL(5,2) DEFAULT 10.0,    -- Can do 10% late
   740тЖТ    
   741тЖТ    -- Certification requirements
   742тЖТ    required_certifications VARCHAR(50)[],
   743тЖТ    
   744тЖТ    -- NEW v2.1: PM type versioning for interval changes over contract life
   745тЖТ    effective_from DATE NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_DATE,
   746тЖТ    effective_to DATE,                              -- NULL = current version
   747тЖТ    superseded_by UUID REFERENCES pm_types(id),    -- Link to newer version
   748тЖТ    
   749тЖТ    -- Canvas display
   750тЖТ    display_color VARCHAR(7),
   751тЖТ    display_priority INTEGER DEFAULT 50,
   752тЖТ    
   753тЖТ    active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
   754тЖТ    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   755тЖТ    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   756тЖТ    
   757тЖТ    UNIQUE(fleet_id, pm_code, version)
   758тЖТ);
   759тЖТ
   760тЖТ-- Index for finding current PM type version
   761тЖТCREATE INDEX idx_pm_types_current ON pm_types(fleet_id, pm_code, effective_from DESC)
   762тЖТ    WHERE effective_to IS NULL AND active = TRUE;
   763тЖТ
   764тЖТ-- Example PM hierarchy data:
   765тЖТ-- INSERT INTO pm_types (fleet_id, pm_code, pm_name, interval_km, interval_months, satisfies_codes, ...)
   766тЖТ-- KS Fleet:
   767тЖТ--   C1: interval_km=12500, interval_months=6,  satisfies_codes=NULL
   768тЖТ--   C2: interval_km=50000, interval_months=24, satisfies_codes=['C1']
   769тЖТ--   C3: interval_km=100000, interval_months=48, satisfies_codes=['C1','C2']
   770тЖТ--   C4: interval_km=200000, interval_months=96, satisfies_codes=['C1','C2','C3']
   771тЖТ--   OH: interval_km=800000, interval_months=NULL, satisfies_codes=['C1','C2','C3','C4']
   772тЖТ
   773тЖТ-- PM material requirements (Bill of Materials)
   774тЖТCREATE TABLE pm_material_requirements (
   775тЖТ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   776тЖТ    pm_type_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES pm_types(id),
   777тЖТ    part_number VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
   778тЖТ    part_description TEXT,
   779тЖТ    
   780тЖТ    quantity_per_pm INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
   781тЖТ    criticality material_criticality NOT NULL DEFAULT 'BLOCKING',
   782тЖТ    -- BLOCKING = cannot start PM without part
   783тЖТ    -- DEFERRABLE = can start, must have within X days
   784тЖТ    -- CONSUMABLE = nice-to-have, can substitute
   785тЖТ    
   786тЖТ    lead_time_days INTEGER DEFAULT 14,
   787тЖТ    substitute_parts VARCHAR(50)[],    -- Alternative part numbers
   788тЖТ    
   789тЖТ    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   790тЖТ    
   791тЖТ    UNIQUE(pm_type_id, part_number)
   792тЖТ);
   793тЖТ
   794тЖТCREATE INDEX idx_pm_materials_type ON pm_material_requirements(pm_type_id);
   795тЖТ
   796тЖТ-- PM schedule (planned and executed maintenance)
   797тЖТCREATE TABLE pm_schedule (
   798тЖТ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   799тЖТ    train_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES trains(id),
   800тЖТ    pm_type_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES pm_types(id),
   801тЖТ    
   802тЖТ    -- Scheduling
   803тЖТ    target_date DATE NOT NULL,
   804тЖТ    target_mileage_km INTEGER,
   805тЖТ    status pm_status DEFAULT 'SCHEDULED',
   806тЖТ    
   807тЖТ    -- Compliance window (calculated from pm_types tolerances)
   808тЖТ    constraint_start DATE NOT NULL,    -- Earliest allowed date
   809тЖТ    constraint_end DATE NOT NULL,      -- Latest allowed date (statutory lock after this)
   810тЖТ    
   811тЖТ    -- Resource assignment
   812тЖТ    assigned_depot_id UUID REFERENCES depots(id),
   813тЖТ    assigned_track_id UUID REFERENCES tracks(id),
   814тЖТ    assigned_hours DECIMAL(6,2),
   815тЖТ    assigned_shift shift_type,
   816тЖТ    
   817тЖТ    -- Execution
   818тЖТ    actual_start_date DATE,
   819тЖТ    actual_end_date DATE,
   820тЖТ    actual_hours DECIMAL(6,2),
   821тЖТ    completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   822тЖТ    completed_mileage_km INTEGER,
   823тЖТ    
   824тЖТ    -- Work order integration
   825тЖТ    maximo_wo_id VARCHAR(50),
   826тЖТ    
   827тЖТ    -- Locking
   828тЖТ    is_locked BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,   -- Cannot be moved by optimizer
   829тЖТ    locked_reason VARCHAR(100),
   830тЖТ    locked_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
   831тЖТ    locked_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   832тЖТ    
   833тЖТ    -- Scenario support (NULL = production schedule)
   834тЖТ    scenario_id UUID REFERENCES scenarios(id),
   835тЖТ    
   836тЖТ    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   837тЖТ    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   838тЖТ    created_by UUID REFERENCES users(id)
   839тЖТ);
   840тЖТ
   841тЖТCREATE INDEX idx_pm_schedule_train ON pm_schedule(train_id, target_date);
   842тЖТCREATE INDEX idx_pm_schedule_date ON pm_schedule(target_date, status);
   843тЖТCREATE INDEX idx_pm_schedule_depot ON pm_schedule(assigned_depot_id, target_date) 
   844тЖТ    WHERE status IN ('SCHEDULED', 'IN_PROGRESS');
   845тЖТCREATE INDEX idx_pm_schedule_scenario ON pm_schedule(scenario_id) WHERE scenario_id IS NOT NULL;
   846тЖТCREATE INDEX idx_pm_schedule_canvas ON pm_schedule(target_date, train_id)
   847тЖТ    WHERE status IN ('SCHEDULED', 'IN_PROGRESS') AND scenario_id IS NULL;
   848тЖТ```

   852тЖТ## 3.6 Material Management Tables
   853тЖТ
   854тЖТ```sql
   855тЖТ-- Inventory positions (snapshot from ERP/Maximo)
   856тЖТCREATE TABLE inventory_positions (
   857тЖТ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   858тЖТ    depot_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES depots(id),
   859тЖТ    part_number VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
   860тЖТ    part_description TEXT,
   861тЖТ    
   862тЖТ    -- Quantities
   863тЖТ    quantity_on_hand INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
   864тЖТ    quantity_reserved INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,  -- Soft + hard reservations
   865тЖТ    quantity_on_order INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
   866тЖТ    quantity_available INTEGER GENERATED ALWAYS AS 
   867тЖТ        (quantity_on_hand - quantity_reserved) STORED,
   868тЖТ    
   869тЖТ    -- Replenishment
   870тЖТ    reorder_point INTEGER,
   871тЖТ    reorder_quantity INTEGER,
   872тЖТ    next_delivery_date DATE,
   873тЖТ    next_delivery_quantity INTEGER,
   874тЖТ    
   875тЖТ    -- Cost
   876тЖТ    unit_cost DECIMAL(12,2),
   877тЖТ    currency VARCHAR(3) DEFAULT 'SGD',
   878тЖТ    
   879тЖТ    -- Sync tracking
   880тЖТ    last_sync_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   881тЖТ    source_system VARCHAR(50) DEFAULT 'MAXIMO',
   882тЖТ    
   883тЖТ    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   884тЖТ    
   885тЖТ    UNIQUE(depot_id, part_number)
   886тЖТ);
   887тЖТ
   888тЖТCREATE INDEX idx_inventory_depot ON inventory_positions(depot_id);
   889тЖТCREATE INDEX idx_inventory_part ON inventory_positions(part_number);
   890тЖТCREATE INDEX idx_inventory_low_stock ON inventory_positions(depot_id, part_number) 
   891тЖТ    WHERE quantity_available <= reorder_point;
   892тЖТ
   893тЖТ-- Material reservations (link inventory to PM schedule)
   894тЖТCREATE TABLE material_reservations (
   895тЖТ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   896тЖТ    pm_schedule_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES pm_schedule(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   897тЖТ    inventory_position_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES inventory_positions(id),
   898тЖТ    part_number VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
   899тЖТ    
   900тЖТ    quantity_reserved INTEGER NOT NULL,
   901тЖТ    reservation_type reservation_type DEFAULT 'SOFT',
   902тЖТ    -- SOFT = tentative, can be released if PM moves
   903тЖТ    -- HARD = committed, parts allocated
   904тЖТ    
   905тЖТ    reserved_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   906тЖТ    reserved_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
   907тЖТ    expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ,  -- SOFT reservations auto-expire
   908тЖТ    
   909тЖТ    -- Fulfillment
   910тЖТ    fulfilled_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   911тЖТ    fulfilled_quantity INTEGER,
   912тЖТ    
   913тЖТ    UNIQUE(pm_schedule_id, part_number)
   914тЖТ);
   915тЖТ
   916тЖТCREATE INDEX idx_reservations_pm ON material_reservations(pm_schedule_id);
   917тЖТCREATE INDEX idx_reservations_inventory ON material_reservations(inventory_position_id);
   918тЖТCREATE INDEX idx_reservations_expiring ON material_reservations(expires_at) 
   919тЖТ    WHERE reservation_type = 'SOFT' AND fulfilled_at IS NULL;
   920тЖТ```
   921тЖТ

   923тЖТ
   924тЖТ## 3.7 Man-hour Planning Tables
   925тЖТ
   926тЖТ```sql
   927тЖТ-- Man-hour capacity (resource availability by depot)
   928тЖТCREATE TABLE manhour_capacity (
   929тЖТ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   930тЖТ    depot_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES depots(id),
   931тЖТ    effective_from DATE NOT NULL,
   932тЖТ    effective_to DATE,
   933тЖТ    
   934тЖТ    -- Base capacity per shift type
   935тЖТ    day_shift_hours DECIMAL(8,2) NOT NULL,
   936тЖТ    night_shift_hours DECIMAL(8,2) DEFAULT 0,
   937тЖТ    weekend_hours DECIMAL(8,2) DEFAULT 0,
   938тЖТ    
   939тЖТ    -- Staff counts
   940тЖТ    technicians_day INTEGER NOT NULL,
   941тЖТ    technicians_night INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
   942тЖТ    specialists INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
   943тЖТ    
   944тЖТ    -- Constraints
   945тЖТ    max_overtime_pct DECIMAL(5,2) DEFAULT 20.0,
   946тЖТ    min_utilization_pct DECIMAL(5,2) DEFAULT 70.0,
   947тЖТ    target_utilization_pct DECIMAL(5,2) DEFAULT 85.0,
   948тЖТ    
   949тЖТ    notes TEXT,
   950тЖТ    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   951тЖТ    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   952тЖТ    
   953тЖТ    UNIQUE(depot_id, effective_from)
   954тЖТ);
   955тЖТ
   956тЖТ-- Man-hour projections (generated by leveling engine)
   957тЖТCREATE TABLE manhour_projections (
   958тЖТ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   959тЖТ    projection_run_id UUID NOT NULL,
   960тЖТ    depot_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES depots(id),
   961тЖТ    
   962тЖТ    period_start DATE NOT NULL,
   963тЖТ    period_end DATE NOT NULL,
   964тЖТ    granularity VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,  -- 'WEEKLY', 'MONTHLY'
   965тЖТ    
   966тЖТ    -- Required hours breakdown
   967тЖТ    required_pm_hours DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,
   968тЖТ    required_corrective_hours DECIMAL(10,2) DEFAULT 0,
   969тЖТ    required_overhaul_hours DECIMAL(10,2) DEFAULT 0,
   970тЖТ    required_total_hours DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,
   971тЖТ    
   972тЖТ    -- Available hours
   973тЖТ    available_regular_hours DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,
   974тЖТ    available_overtime_hours DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,
   975тЖТ    available_total_hours DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,
   976тЖТ    
   977тЖТ    -- Computed metrics
   978тЖТ    variance_hours DECIMAL(10,2) GENERATED ALWAYS AS 
   979тЖТ        (available_total_hours - required_total_hours) STORED,
   980тЖТ    utilization_pct DECIMAL(5,2) GENERATED ALWAYS AS 
   981тЖТ        (CASE WHEN available_total_hours > 0 
   982тЖТ              THEN (required_total_hours / available_total_hours * 100)
   983тЖТ              ELSE 0 END) STORED,
   984тЖТ    is_overloaded BOOLEAN GENERATED ALWAYS AS 
   985тЖТ        (required_total_hours > available_total_hours) STORED,
   986тЖТ    is_underutilized BOOLEAN GENERATED ALWAYS AS 
   987тЖТ        (required_total_hours < available_total_hours * 0.7) STORED,
   988тЖТ    
   989тЖТ    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
   990тЖТ);
   991тЖТ
   992тЖТCREATE INDEX idx_manhour_proj_depot ON manhour_projections(depot_id, period_start);
   993тЖТCREATE INDEX idx_manhour_proj_run ON manhour_projections(projection_run_id);
   994тЖТ
   995тЖТ-- Man-hour adjustments (proposed changes to level workload)
   996тЖТCREATE TABLE manhour_adjustments (
   997тЖТ    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   998тЖТ    projection_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES manhour_projections(id),
   999тЖТ    
  1000тЖТ    adjustment_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
  1001тЖТ    -- 'ADD_OVERTIME', 'DEFER_PM', 'ADVANCE_PM', 'CROSS_DEPOT', 'ADD_CONTRACTOR'
  1002тЖТ    
  1003тЖТ    source_period_start DATE,
  1004тЖТ    source_period_end DATE,
  1005тЖТ    target_period_start DATE,
  1006тЖТ    target_period_end DATE,
  1007тЖТ    hours_adjusted DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,
  1008тЖТ    
  1009тЖТ    affected_pm_ids UUID[] DEFAULT '{}',
  1010тЖТ    affected_train_ids UUID[] DEFAULT '{}',
  1011тЖТ    
  1012тЖТ    -- Impact assessment
  1013тЖТ    cost_impact DECIMAL(12,2),
  1014тЖТ    risk_impact VARCHAR(20),  -- 'NONE', 'LOW', 'MEDIUM', 'HIGH'
  1015тЖТ    risk_description TEXT,
  1016тЖТ    
  1017тЖТ    -- Workflow
  1018тЖТ    status adjustment_status DEFAULT 'PROPOSED',
  1019тЖТ    proposed_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
  1020тЖТ    proposed_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
  1021тЖТ    approved_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
  1022тЖТ    approved_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  1023тЖТ    rejection_reason TEXT,
  1024тЖТ    
  1025тЖТ    -- Explanation
  1026тЖТ    reason TEXT NOT NULL,
  1027тЖТ    
  1028тЖТ    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
  1029тЖТ);
  1030тЖТ
  1031тЖТCREATE INDEX idx_manhour_adj_projection ON manhour_adjustments(projection_id);
  1032тЖТCREATE INDEX idx_manhour_adj_status ON manhour_adjustments(status) WHERE status = 'PROPOSED';
  1033тЖТ```
