  1257тЖТ
  1258тЖТ---
  1259тЖТ
  1260тЖТ## 5.14 WebSocket Events
  1261тЖТ
  1262тЖТ### Connection
  1263тЖТ
  1264тЖТ```javascript
  1265тЖТconst ws = new WebSocket('wss://mms.example.com/ws');
  1266тЖТ
  1267тЖТws.onopen = () => {
  1268тЖТ  ws.send(JSON.stringify({
  1269тЖТ    type: 'AUTH',
  1270тЖТ    token: 'eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIs...'
  1271тЖТ  }));
  1272тЖТ};
  1273тЖТ```
  1274тЖТ
  1275тЖТ### Event Types
  1276тЖТ
  1277тЖТ| Event | Direction | Description |
  1278тЖТ|-------|-----------|-------------|
  1279тЖТ| `canvas:pm_updated` | Server├втАатАЩClient | PM schedule changed |
  1280тЖТ| `canvas:pm_locked` | Server├втАатАЩClient | PM locked/unlocked |
  1281тЖТ| `canvas:manhour_updated` | Server├втАатАЩClient | Man-hour projections updated |
  1282тЖТ| `canvas:drift_updated` | Server├втАатАЩClient | Drift analysis updated |
  1283тЖТ| `optimizer:progress` | Server├втАатАЩClient | Optimizer job progress |
  1284тЖТ| `optimizer:complete` | Server├втАатАЩClient | Optimizer job completed |
  1285тЖТ| `canvas:subscribe` | Client├втАатАЩServer | Subscribe to canvas region |
  1286тЖТ| `canvas:unsubscribe` | Client├втАатАЩServer | Unsubscribe from canvas region |
  1287тЖТ| `canvas:move_pm` | Client├втАатАЩServer | Request PM move (drag-and-drop) |
  1288тЖТ
  1289тЖТ### Event Payloads
  1290тЖТ
  1291тЖТ```typescript
  1292тЖТ// PM Updated Event
  1293тЖТinterface PMUpdatedEvent {
  1294тЖТ  type: 'canvas:pm_updated';
  1295тЖТ  data: {
  1296тЖТ    pmId: string;
  1297тЖТ    trainId: string;
  1298тЖТ    changes: {
  1299тЖТ      field: string;
  1300тЖТ      oldValue: any;
  1301тЖТ      newValue: any;
  1302тЖТ    }[];
  1303тЖТ    changedBy: {
  1304тЖТ      userId: string;
  1305тЖТ      displayName: string;
  1306тЖТ    };
  1307тЖТ  };
  1308тЖТ}
  1309тЖТ
  1310тЖТ// Optimizer Progress Event
  1311тЖТinterface OptimizerProgressEvent {
  1312тЖТ  type: 'optimizer:progress';
  1313тЖТ  data: {
  1314тЖТ    jobId: string;
  1315тЖТ    percent: number;
  1316тЖТ    currentObjective: number;
  1317тЖТ    feasible: boolean;
  1318тЖТ    elapsedMs: number;
  1319тЖТ  };
  1320тЖТ}
  1321тЖТ
  1322тЖТ// Canvas Subscribe Request
  1323тЖТinterface CanvasSubscribeRequest {
  1324тЖТ  type: 'canvas:subscribe';
  1325тЖТ  data: {
  1326тЖТ    region: {
  1327тЖТ      trainIds?: string[];
  1328тЖТ      fromDate: string;
  1329тЖТ      toDate: string;
  1330тЖТ      zoomLevel: 'STRATEGIC' | 'TACTICAL' | 'OPERATIONAL';
  1331тЖТ    };
  1332тЖТ  };
  1333тЖТ}
  1334тЖТ
  1335тЖТ// PM Move Request (Drag-and-Drop)
  1336тЖТinterface MovePMRequest {
  1337тЖТ  type: 'canvas:move_pm';
  1338тЖТ  data: {
  1339тЖТ    pmId: string;
  1340тЖТ    newTargetDate: string;  // ISO date
  1341тЖТ    newTrackId?: string;    // Optional track reassignment
  1342тЖТ  };
  1343тЖТ}
  1344тЖТ
  1345тЖТ// PM Move Response
  1346тЖТinterface MovePMResponse {
  1347тЖТ  type: 'canvas:move_pm_result';
  1348тЖТ  data: {
  1349тЖТ    success: boolean;
  1350тЖТ    pmId: string;
  1351тЖТ    newTargetDate?: string;
  1352тЖТ    error?: {
  1353тЖТ      code: 'MAXIMO_LOCK' | 'OUTSIDE_WINDOW' | 'TRACK_CONFLICT' | 'MATERIAL_BLOCK' | 'BASELINE_LOCK';
  1354тЖТ      message: string;
  1355тЖТ      suggestion?: string;
  1356тЖТ      maximoStatus?: string;
  1357тЖТ    };
  1358тЖТ  };
  1359тЖТ}
  1360тЖТ```
  1361тЖТ
  1362тЖТ### Canvas Move PM Handler
  1363тЖТ
  1364тЖТ**CRITICAL:** All PM movements MUST validate against Maximo status to prevent race conditions with in-progress work orders.
  1365тЖТ
  1366тЖТ```typescript
  1367тЖТ// WebSocket handler for canvas:move_pm
  1368тЖТasync function handleMovePM(
  1369тЖТ  ws: WebSocket, 
  1370тЖТ  request: MovePMRequest,
  1371тЖТ  userId: string
  1372тЖТ): Promise<void> {
  1373тЖТ  const { pmId, newTargetDate, newTrackId } = request.data;
  1374тЖТ  
  1375тЖТ  try {
  1376тЖТ    // 1. CRITICAL: Validate Maximo status lock
  1377тЖТ    // This prevents rescheduling PMs that technicians are actively working on
  1378тЖТ    const editValidation = await pmEditValidator.validateEdit(pmId);
  1379тЖТ    
  1380тЖТ    if (!editValidation.valid) {
  1381тЖТ      ws.send(JSON.stringify({
  1382тЖТ        type: 'canvas:move_pm_result',
  1383тЖТ        data: {
  1384тЖТ          success: false,
  1385тЖТ          pmId,
  1386тЖТ          error: {
  1387тЖТ            code: editValidation.reason === 'MAXIMO_STATUS_LOCK' ? 'MAXIMO_LOCK' : 
  1388тЖТ                  editValidation.reason === 'BASELINE_LOCK' ? 'BASELINE_LOCK' : 'MAXIMO_LOCK',
  1389тЖТ            message: editValidation.message,
  1390тЖТ            suggestion: editValidation.suggestion,
  1391тЖТ            maximoStatus: editValidation.maximoStatus
  1392тЖТ          }
  1393тЖТ        }
  1394тЖТ      }));
  1395тЖТ      return;
  1396тЖТ    }
  1397тЖТ    
  1398тЖТ    // 2. Validate compliance window
  1399тЖТ    const pm = await pmScheduleService.getPM(pmId);
  1400тЖТ    const targetDate = new Date(newTargetDate);
  1401тЖТ    
  1402тЖТ    if (targetDate < pm.constraintStart || targetDate > pm.constraintEnd) {
  1403тЖТ      ws.send(JSON.stringify({
  1404тЖТ        type: 'canvas:move_pm_result',
  1405тЖТ        data: {
  1406тЖТ          success: false,
  1407тЖТ          pmId,
  1408тЖТ          error: {
  1409тЖТ            code: 'OUTSIDE_WINDOW',
  1410тЖТ            message: `Date must be between ${pm.constraintStart.toISOString().split('T')[0]} and ${pm.constraintEnd.toISOString().split('T')[0]}`,
  1411тЖТ            suggestion: 'Drag within the highlighted compliance window'
  1412тЖТ          }
  1413тЖТ        }
  1414тЖТ      }));
  1415тЖТ      return;
  1416тЖТ    }
  1417тЖТ    
  1418тЖТ    // 3. Validate track availability (if track specified)
  1419тЖТ    if (newTrackId) {
  1420тЖТ      const trackConflict = await trackService.checkConflict(
  1421тЖТ        newTrackId, 
  1422тЖТ        targetDate, 
  1423тЖТ        pm.pmType.minTrackDays
  1424тЖТ      );
  1425тЖТ      
  1426тЖТ      if (trackConflict) {
  1427тЖТ        ws.send(JSON.stringify({
  1428тЖТ          type: 'canvas:move_pm_result',
  1429тЖТ          data: {
  1430тЖТ            success: false,
  1431тЖТ            pmId,
  1432тЖТ            error: {
  1433тЖТ              code: 'TRACK_CONFLICT',
  1434тЖТ              message: `Track ${trackConflict.trackNumber} occupied by ${trackConflict.conflictingPM}`,
  1435тЖТ              suggestion: 'Select a different track or date'
  1436тЖТ            }
  1437тЖТ          }
  1438тЖТ        }));
  1439тЖТ        return;
  1440тЖТ      }
  1441тЖТ    }
  1442тЖТ    
  1443тЖТ    // 4. Apply the move
  1444тЖТ    await pmScheduleService.movePM(pmId, targetDate, newTrackId, userId);
  1445тЖТ    
  1446тЖТ    // 5. Broadcast update to all connected clients
  1447тЖТ    broadcastToRoom('canvas', {
  1448тЖТ      type: 'canvas:pm_updated',
  1449тЖТ      data: {
  1450тЖТ        pmId,
  1451тЖТ        trainId: pm.trainId,
  1452тЖТ        changes: [{ field: 'targetDate', oldValue: pm.targetDate, newValue: newTargetDate }],
  1453тЖТ        changedBy: { userId, displayName: await getUserDisplayName(userId) }
  1454тЖТ      }
  1455тЖТ    });
  1456тЖТ    
  1457тЖТ    // 6. Send success response
  1458тЖТ    ws.send(JSON.stringify({
  1459тЖТ      type: 'canvas:move_pm_result',
  1460тЖТ      data: { success: true, pmId, newTargetDate }
  1461тЖТ    }));
  1462тЖТ    
  1463тЖТ    // 7. Queue Maximo sync (async)
  1464тЖТ    await maximoSyncQueue.add({ pmId, action: 'UPDATE' });
  1465тЖТ    
  1466тЖТ  } catch (error) {
  1467тЖТ    logger.error({ pmId, error }, 'PM move failed');
  1468тЖТ    ws.send(JSON.stringify({
  1469тЖТ      type: 'canvas:move_pm_result',
  1470тЖТ      data: {
  1471тЖТ        success: false,
  1472тЖТ        pmId,
  1473тЖТ        error: { code: 'INTERNAL_ERROR', message: 'Failed to move PM' }
  1474тЖТ      }
  1475тЖТ    }));
  1476тЖТ  }
  1477тЖТ}
  1478тЖТ```
  1479тЖТ
  1480тЖТ---
  1481тЖТ
  1482тЖТ## 5.15 Export Endpoints
  1483тЖТ
  1484тЖТ### POST /export/pm-schedule
  1485тЖТ
  1486тЖТExport PM schedule to Excel.
  1487тЖТ
  1488тЖТ**Request:**
  1489тЖТ```json
  1490тЖТ{
  1491тЖТ  "format": "xlsx",
  1492тЖТ  "filters": {
  1493тЖТ    "depotId": "depot-bishan",
  1494тЖТ    "fromDate": "2026-01-01",
  1495тЖТ    "toDate": "2026-12-31"
  1496тЖТ  },
  1497тЖТ  "columns": ["trainNumber", "pmCode", "targetDate", "status", "assignedTrack"]
  1498тЖТ}
  1499тЖТ```
  1500тЖТ
  1501тЖТ**Response (202):**
  1502тЖТ```json
  1503тЖТ{
  1504тЖТ  "success": true,
  1505тЖТ  "data": {
  1506тЖТ    "jobId": "export-001",
  1507тЖТ    "status": "PROCESSING",
  1508тЖТ    "estimatedSizeBytes": 150000
  1509тЖТ  }
  1510тЖТ}
  1511тЖТ```
  1512тЖТ
  1513тЖТ### GET /export/:jobId
  1514тЖТ
  1515тЖТGet export job status and download URL.
  1516тЖТ
  1517тЖТ**Response (200):**
  1518тЖТ```json
  1519тЖТ{
  1520тЖТ  "success": true,
  1521тЖТ  "data": {
  1522тЖТ    "jobId": "export-001",
  1523тЖТ    "status": "COMPLETED",
  1524тЖТ    "downloadUrl": "https://mms.example.com/downloads/export-001.xlsx",
  1525тЖТ    "expiresAt": "2026-01-15T10:00:00Z",
  1526тЖТ    "fileSizeBytes": 145320
  1527тЖТ  }
  1528тЖТ}
  1529тЖТ```
  1530тЖТ
  1531тЖТ### POST /export/report
  1532тЖТ
  1533тЖТGenerate PDF report.
  1534тЖТ
  1535тЖТ**Request:**
  1536тЖТ```json
  1537тЖТ{
  1538тЖТ  "reportType": "WEEKLY_COMPLIANCE",
  1539тЖТ  "parameters": {
  1540тЖТ    "depotId": "depot-bishan",
  1541тЖТ    "weekOf": "2026-01-13"
  1542тЖТ  }
  1543тЖТ}
  1544тЖТ```
  1545тЖТ

# Metro Fleet Mileage Management System
# Part 5: API Specification

**Document Version:** 2.0  
**Status:** APPROVED FOR DEVELOPMENT

---

## 5.1 API Overview

### 5.1.1 Base Configuration

```yaml
Base URL: /api/v1
Content-Type: application/json
Authentication: Bearer JWT
Rate Limiting: 100 requests/minute per user
```

### 5.1.2 Standard Response Format

```typescript
// Success Response
interface SuccessResponse<T> {
  success: true;
  data: T;
  meta?: {
    pagination?: PaginationMeta;
    timing?: { durationMs: number };
  };
}

// Error Response
interface ErrorResponse {
  success: false;
  error: {
    code: string;           // e.g., 'VALIDATION_ERROR', 'NOT_FOUND'
    message: string;        // Human-readable message
    details?: any;          // Additional error context
    requestId: string;      // For support/debugging
  };
}

// Pagination
interface PaginationMeta {
  page: number;
  pageSize: number;
  totalItems: number;
  totalPages: number;
  hasNext: boolean;
  hasPrev: boolean;
}
```

### 5.1.3 Standard Query Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `page` | number | Page number (1-indexed) |
| `pageSize` | number | Items per page (default: 50, max: 200) |
| `sort` | string | Sort field with direction (e.g., `targetDate:asc`) |
| `filter` | string | JSON-encoded filter object |
| `fields` | string | Comma-separated fields to include |

## 5.14 WebSocket Events

### Connection

```javascript
const ws = new WebSocket('wss://mms.example.com/ws');

ws.onopen = () => {
  ws.send(JSON.stringify({
    type: 'AUTH',
    token: 'eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIs...'
  }));
};
```

### Event Types

| Event | Direction | Description |
|-------|-----------|-------------|
| `canvas:pm_updated` | Server├втАатАЩClient | PM schedule changed |
| `canvas:pm_locked` | Server├втАатАЩClient | PM locked/unlocked |
| `canvas:manhour_updated` | Server├втАатАЩClient | Man-hour projections updated |
| `canvas:drift_updated` | Server├втАатАЩClient | Drift analysis updated |
| `optimizer:progress` | Server├втАатАЩClient | Optimizer job progress |
| `optimizer:complete` | Server├втАатАЩClient | Optimizer job completed |
| `canvas:subscribe` | Client├втАатАЩServer | Subscribe to canvas region |
| `canvas:unsubscribe` | Client├втАатАЩServer | Unsubscribe from canvas region |
| `canvas:move_pm` | Client├втАатАЩServer | Request PM move (drag-and-drop) |

### Event Payloads

```typescript
// PM Updated Event
interface PMUpdatedEvent {
  type: 'canvas:pm_updated';
  data: {
    pmId: string;
    trainId: string;
    changes: {
      field: string;
      oldValue: any;
      newValue: any;
    }[];
    changedBy: {
      userId: string;
      displayName: string;
    };
  };
}

// Optimizer Progress Event
interface OptimizerProgressEvent {
  type: 'optimizer:progress';
  data: {
    jobId: string;
    percent: number;
    currentObjective: number;
    feasible: boolean;
    elapsedMs: number;
  };
}

// Canvas Subscribe Request
interface CanvasSubscribeRequest {
  type: 'canvas:subscribe';
  data: {
    region: {
      trainIds?: string[];
      fromDate: string;
      toDate: string;
      zoomLevel: 'STRATEGIC' | 'TACTICAL' | 'OPERATIONAL';
    };
  };
}

// PM Move Request (Drag-and-Drop)
interface MovePMRequest {
  type: 'canvas:move_pm';
  data: {
    pmId: string;
    newTargetDate: string;  // ISO date
    newTrackId?: string;    // Optional track reassignment
  };
}

// PM Move Response
interface MovePMResponse {
  type: 'canvas:move_pm_result';
  data: {
    success: boolean;
    pmId: string;
    newTargetDate?: string;
    error?: {
      code: 'MAXIMO_LOCK' | 'OUTSIDE_WINDOW' | 'TRACK_CONFLICT' | 'MATERIAL_BLOCK' | 'BASELINE_LOCK';
      message: string;
      suggestion?: string;
      maximoStatus?: string;
    };
  };
}
```

### Canvas Move PM Handler

**CRITICAL:** All PM movements MUST validate against Maximo status to prevent race conditions with in-progress work orders.

```typescript
// WebSocket handler for canvas:move_pm
async function handleMovePM(
  ws: WebSocket, 
  request: MovePMRequest,
  userId: string
): Promise<void> {
  const { pmId, newTargetDate, newTrackId } = request.data;
  
  try {
    // 1. CRITICAL: Validate Maximo status lock
    // This prevents rescheduling PMs that technicians are actively working on
    const editValidation = await pmEditValidator.validateEdit(pmId);
    
    if (!editValidation.valid) {
      ws.send(JSON.stringify({
        type: 'canvas:move_pm_result',
        data: {
          success: false,
          pmId,
          error: {
            code: editValidation.reason === 'MAXIMO_STATUS_LOCK' ? 'MAXIMO_LOCK' : 
                  editValidation.reason === 'BASELINE_LOCK' ? 'BASELINE_LOCK' : 'MAXIMO_LOCK',
            message: editValidation.message,
            suggestion: editValidation.suggestion,
            maximoStatus: editValidation.maximoStatus
          }
        }
      }));
      return;
    }
    
    // 2. Validate compliance window
    const pm = await pmScheduleService.getPM(pmId);
    const targetDate = new Date(newTargetDate);
    
    if (targetDate < pm.constraintStart || targetDate > pm.constraintEnd) {
      ws.send(JSON.stringify({
        type: 'canvas:move_pm_result',
        data: {
          success: false,
          pmId,
          error: {
            code: 'OUTSIDE_WINDOW',
            message: `Date must be between ${pm.constraintStart.toISOString().split('T')[0]} and ${pm.constraintEnd.toISOString().split('T')[0]}`,
            suggestion: 'Drag within the highlighted compliance window'
          }
        }
      }));
      return;
    }
    
    // 3. Validate track availability (if track specified)
    if (newTrackId) {
      const trackConflict = await trackService.checkConflict(
        newTrackId, 
        targetDate, 
        pm.pmType.minTrackDays
      );
      
      if (trackConflict) {
        ws.send(JSON.stringify({
          type: 'canvas:move_pm_result',
          data: {
            success: false,
            pmId,
            error: {
              code: 'TRACK_CONFLICT',
              message: `Track ${trackConflict.trackNumber} occupied by ${trackConflict.conflictingPM}`,
              suggestion: 'Select a different track or date'
            }
          }
        }));
        return;
      }
    }
    
    // 4. Apply the move
    await pmScheduleService.movePM(pmId, targetDate, newTrackId, userId);
    
    // 5. Broadcast update to all connected clients
    broadcastToRoom('canvas', {
      type: 'canvas:pm_updated',
      data: {
        pmId,
        trainId: pm.trainId,
        changes: [{ field: 'targetDate', oldValue: pm.targetDate, newValue: newTargetDate }],
        changedBy: { userId, displayName: await getUserDisplayName(userId) }
      }
    });
    
    // 6. Send success response
    ws.send(JSON.stringify({
      type: 'canvas:move_pm_result',
      data: { success: true, pmId, newTargetDate }
    }));
    
    // 7. Queue Maximo sync (async)
    await maximoSyncQueue.add({ pmId, action: 'UPDATE' });
    
  } catch (error) {
    logger.error({ pmId, error }, 'PM move failed');
    ws.send(JSON.stringify({
      type: 'canvas:move_pm_result',
      data: {
        success: false,
        pmId,
        error: { code: 'INTERNAL_ERROR', message: 'Failed to move PM' }
      }
    }));
  }
}
```

## 5.15 Export Endpoints

### POST /export/pm-schedule

Export PM schedule to Excel.

**Request:**
```json
{
  "format": "xlsx",
  "filters": {
    "depotId": "depot-bishan",
    "fromDate": "2026-01-01",
    "toDate": "2026-12-31"
  },
  "columns": ["trainNumber", "pmCode", "targetDate", "status", "assignedTrack"]
}
```

**Response (202):**
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "jobId": "export-001",
    "status": "PROCESSING",
    "estimatedSizeBytes": 150000
  }
}
```

### GET /export/:jobId

Get export job status and download URL.

**Response (200):**
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "jobId": "export-001",
    "status": "COMPLETED",
    "downloadUrl": "https://mms.example.com/downloads/export-001.xlsx",
    "expiresAt": "2026-01-15T10:00:00Z",
    "fileSizeBytes": 145320
  }
}
```

### POST /export/report

Generate PDF report.

**Request:**
```json
{
  "reportType": "WEEKLY_COMPLIANCE",
  "parameters": {
    "depotId": "depot-bishan",
    "weekOf": "2026-01-13"
  }
}
```

    95тЖТ## 2.2 Component Responsibilities
    96тЖТ
    97тЖТ### 2.2.1 Frontend (Infinite Canvas SPA)
    98тЖТ
    99тЖТ| Responsibility | Implementation |
   100тЖТ|----------------|----------------|
   101тЖТ| Timeline rendering | deck.gl WebGL layers with semantic zoom |
   102тЖТ| Strategic view | Heatmap showing PM density per train per year |
   103тЖТ| Tactical view | Gantt blocks for 12-week PM schedule |
   104тЖТ| Operational view | Detail cards with technician, track, duration |
   105тЖТ| Baseline/drift overlay | Shadow layer showing deviation from published plan |
   106тЖТ| Constraint walls | Visual barriers at compliance window boundaries |
   107тЖТ| Drag-and-drop rescheduling | Real-time validation with constraint feedback |
   108тЖТ| Man-hour visualization | Column overlay showing utilization with/without adjustments |
   109тЖТ| Scenario comparison | Side-by-side diff view for what-if analysis |
   110тЖТ| Real-time collaboration | WebSocket sync for multi-user canvas editing |
   111тЖТ
   112тЖТ### 2.2.2 Backend API (Bun + Hono)
   113тЖТ
   114тЖТ| Responsibility | Implementation |
   115тЖТ|----------------|----------------|
   116тЖТ| REST API | `/api/v1/*` endpoints with OpenAPI spec |
   117тЖТ| WebSocket hub | Real-time canvas updates and collaboration |
   118тЖТ| Authentication | JWT with refresh tokens, RBAC enforcement |
   119тЖТ| Business logic | Orchestration layer for domain services |
   120тЖТ| Task dispatch | BullMQ job queuing for async operations |
   121тЖТ| Optimizer IPC | Stream-based communication with C++ binary |
   122тЖТ| Audit logging | Immutable change records with user attribution |
   123тЖТ
   124тЖТ### 2.2.3 File Watcher Service
   125тЖТ
   126тЖТ| Responsibility | Implementation |
   127тЖТ|----------------|----------------|
   128тЖТ| Folder monitoring | chokidar watching `/data/inbox/` |
   129тЖТ| File validation | Format, size, checksum verification |
   130тЖТ| Queue dispatch | BullMQ job creation for ingestion worker |
   131тЖТ| Archive management | Move processed files to `/data/processed/` |
   132тЖТ| Quarantine handling | Move invalid files to `/data/quarantine/` |
   133тЖТ| Health alerting | Notification when files stop arriving (>2 hours) |
   134тЖТ
   135тЖТ### 2.2.4 BullMQ Workers
   136тЖТ
   137тЖТ| Worker | Responsibility | Concurrency |
   138тЖТ|--------|----------------|-------------|
   139тЖТ| `ingestion_worker` | Parse CSV, validate rows, insert to DB, handle meter swaps | 2 |
   140тЖТ| `optimizer_worker` | Spawn C++ binary, stream I/O, store results with explanations | 1 per depot |
   141тЖТ| `tactical_scheduler` | 12-week PM scheduling with material checks | 1 |
   142тЖТ| `strategic_planner` | 1-10 year capacity reservation with constraint envelope | 1 |
   143тЖТ| `manhour_leveling_worker` | Weekly/monthly workload balancing, adjustment generation | 1 |
   144тЖТ| `drift_monitor_worker` | Nightly baseline comparison, threshold alerting | 1 |
   145тЖТ| `report_worker` | Generate PDF/Excel reports | 2 |
   146тЖТ| `notification_worker` | Send email alerts and notifications | 3 |
   147тЖТ
   148тЖТ### 2.2.5 C++ Optimizer Binary
   149тЖТ
   150тЖТ| Responsibility | Implementation |
   151тЖТ|----------------|----------------|
   152тЖТ| Constraint solving | OR-Tools CP-SAT with soft constraint penalties |
   153тЖТ| Explanation generation | Factor-based trace for each assignment decision |
   154тЖТ| Progress streaming | NDJSON messages to stdout during solve |
   155тЖТ| Metrics emission | OpenTelemetry format to stderr |
   156тЖТ| Fallback mode | Greedy heuristic when CP-SAT times out |
   157тЖТ| Material validation | Check inventory positions for blocking parts |
   158тЖТ
   159тЖТ---
   160тЖТ
   161тЖТ## 2.3 Critical Data Flows
   162тЖТ
   163тЖТ### 2.3.1 Daily Operational Flow
   164тЖТ
   165тЖТ```
   166тЖТ06:00 тФВ CSV file drop (mileage.csv) тЖТ File Watcher detects
   167тЖТ06:05 тФВ Ingestion Worker: Parse, validate, insert mileage readings
   168тЖТ06:10 тФВ Ingestion Worker: Update train current_mileage, detect anomalies
   169тЖТ06:15 тФВ Drift Monitor: Check against baseline, flag significant deviations
   170тЖТ06:30 тФВ Optimizer Worker: Generate today's run assignments
   171тЖТ      тФВ   - Input: trains, routes, constraints, inventory positions
   172тЖТ      тФВ   - Output: assignments with explanation traces
   173тЖТ07:00 тФВ WebSocket: Push results to connected canvas clients
   174тЖТ07:00 тФВ Operators: Review assignments on Infinite Canvas
   175тЖТ      тФВ   - View explanation factors for each assignment
   176тЖТ      тФВ   - Drag-and-drop to override if needed (with audit trail)
   177тЖТ08:00 тФВ Export: Approved assignments sent to OCC system
   178тЖТ16:00 тФВ Actual mileage updates (if available intra-day)
   179тЖТ22:00 тФВ Optimizer Worker: Generate tomorrow's assignments
   180тЖТ```
   181тЖТ
   182тЖТ### 2.3.2 Weekly Planning Flow
   183тЖТ
   184тЖТ```
   185тЖТMonday 06:00  тФВ Strategic Planner: Run 10-year horizon update
   186тЖТ              тФВ   - Uses constraint envelope from tactical model
   187тЖТ              тФВ   - Respects published baseline (drift threshold)
   188тЖТMonday 06:30  тФВ Tactical Scheduler: Run 12-week PM scheduling
   189тЖТ              тФВ   - Check material availability for T+14 days (hard)
   190тЖТ              тФВ   - Check material availability for T+90 days (soft)
   191тЖТMonday 07:00  тФВ Man-hour Leveling: Generate 12-week projection
   192тЖТ              тФВ   - Identify overloaded/underutilized weeks
   193тЖТ              тФВ   - Propose adjustments (defer/advance/overtime)
   194тЖТMonday 08:00  тФВ WebSocket: Push updated canvas data to all clients
   195тЖТTuesday       тФВ Planners: Review PM conflicts on canvas
   196тЖТ              тФВ   - Drag to resolve conflicts (audit trail)
   197тЖТ              тФВ   - Review/approve man-hour adjustments
   198тЖТWednesday    тФВ Report Worker: Generate resource utilization report
   199тЖТFriday       тФВ Notification Worker: Send weekly compliance report
   200тЖТ```
   201тЖТ
   202тЖТ### 2.3.3 Baseline Publishing Flow
   203тЖТ
   204тЖТ```
   205тЖТUser Action  тФВ Planner clicks "Publish Baseline" on canvas
   206тЖТ             тФВ   - System validates: no critical conflicts
   207тЖТ             тФВ   - System prompts: "This will create Baseline v3.2"
   208тЖТAPI Call     тФВ POST /api/v1/baselines
   209тЖТ             тФВ   - Snapshot current pm_schedule to baseline_pm_schedule
   210тЖТ             тФВ   - Snapshot mileage projections to baseline_mileage_projection
   211тЖТ             тФВ   - Set previous baseline status to SUPERSEDED
   212тЖТ             тФВ   - Set new baseline status to PUBLISHED
   213тЖТPost-Publish тФВ Drift Monitor: Now compares against new baseline
   214тЖТ             тФВ Canvas: Shows drift layer relative to new baseline
   215тЖТ```
   216тЖТ
   217тЖТ### 2.3.4 Scenario Comparison Flow
   218тЖТ
   219тЖТ```
   220тЖТUser Action  тФВ Planner clicks "Create Scenario" on canvas
   221тЖТ             тФВ   - System forks from current published baseline
   222тЖТ             тФВ   - New scenario_id assigned
   223тЖТEditing      тФВ Planner makes changes within scenario
   224тЖТ             тФВ   - All changes stored as deltas (event-sourced)
   225тЖТ             тФВ   - Original baseline data unchanged
   226тЖТComparison   тФВ Planner clicks "Compare Scenarios"
   227тЖТ             тФВ   - System materializes both scenarios
   228тЖТ             тФВ   - Canvas shows side-by-side diff view
   229тЖТ             тФВ   - Metrics: cost impact, resource utilization delta
   230тЖТPromotion    тФВ Planner clicks "Promote to Baseline"
   231тЖТ             тФВ   - Deltas applied to create new baseline
   232тЖТ             тФВ   - Scenario marked as PROMOTED
   233тЖТ```
   234тЖТ
   235тЖТ---
   236тЖТ
   237тЖТ## 2.4 C++ Optimizer Stream Protocol
   238тЖТ
   239тЖТ### 2.4.1 Stream Architecture
   240тЖТ
   241тЖТ```
   242тЖТтФМтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФР         NDJSON Stream          тФМтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФР
   243тЖТтФВ                 тФВ тФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтЦ║ тФВ                 тФВ
   244тЖТтФВ   Bun Worker    тФВ         stdin (input)          тФВ  mms-optimizer  тФВ
   245тЖТтФВ   (TypeScript)  тФВ                                тФВ     (C++)       тФВ
   246тЖТтФВ                 тФВ тЧДтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФА тФВ                 тФВ
   247тЖТтФВ                 тФВ    stdout (results, NDJSON)    тФВ                 тФВ
   248тЖТтФВ                 тФВ тЧДтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФА тФВ                 тФВ
   249тЖТтФВ                 тФВ    stderr (logs, metrics)      тФВ                 тФВ
   250тЖТтФФтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФШ                                тФФтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФШ
   251тЖТ```
   252тЖТ
   253тЖТ### 2.4.2 Input Message Schema
   254тЖТ
   255тЖТ```typescript
   256тЖТinterface OptimizerInput {
   257тЖТ  type: 'SOLVE_REQUEST';
   258тЖТ  request_id: string;  // UUID for correlation
   259тЖТ  config: {
   260тЖТ    max_solve_time_seconds: number;  // Default: 30
   261тЖТ    num_workers: number;             // Default: 4
   262тЖТ    soft_constraint_penalties: {
   263тЖТ      mileage_imbalance: number;     // Default: 100
   264тЖТ      preference_violation: number;  // Default: 50
   265тЖТ      overtime_hours: number;        // Default: 200
   266тЖТ    };
   267тЖТ    explanation_verbosity: 'MINIMAL' | 'STANDARD' | 'DETAILED';
   268тЖТ  };
   269тЖТ  
   270тЖТ  trains: {
   271тЖТ    id: string;
   272тЖТ    train_number: string;
   273тЖТ    fleet_id: string;
   274тЖТ    current_mileage: number;
   275тЖТ    target_mileage: number;  // For balancing calculation
   276тЖТ    fitness_status: 'FIT' | 'RESTRICTED' | 'GROUNDED';
   277тЖТ    restrictions: string[];  // e.g., ['NO_EXPRESS', 'MAX_SPEED_60']
   278тЖТ    home_depot_id: string;
   279тЖТ    current_depot_id: string;
   280тЖТ  }[];
   281тЖТ  
   282тЖТ  routes: {
   283тЖТ    id: string;
   284тЖТ    route_code: string;
   285тЖТ    expected_km: number;
   286тЖТ    required_trains: number;
   287тЖТ    allowed_fleets: string[];
   288тЖТ    restrictions: string[];  // e.g., ['REQUIRES_ATO', 'LONG_PLATFORM']
   289тЖТ  }[];
   290тЖТ  
   291тЖТ  // Hard constraints (must not violate)
   292тЖТ  hard_constraints: {
   293тЖТ    type: 'PM_DUE' | 'GROUNDED' | 'DEPOT_LOCK' | 'CERTIFICATION';
   294тЖТ    train_id: string;
   295тЖТ    description: string;
   296тЖТ  }[];
   297тЖТ  
   298тЖТ  // Soft constraints (minimize violations)
   299тЖТ  soft_constraints: {
   300тЖТ    type: 'MILEAGE_BALANCE' | 'DEPOT_PREFERENCE' | 'ROUTE_PREFERENCE';
   301тЖТ    train_id: string;
   302тЖТ    penalty_weight: number;
   303тЖТ    description: string;
   304тЖТ  }[];
   305тЖТ  
   306тЖТ  // Material availability (NEW)
   307тЖТ  material_positions: {
   308тЖТ    depot_id: string;
   309тЖТ    part_number: string;
   310тЖТ    quantity_available: number;  // on_hand - reserved
   311тЖТ    next_delivery_date: string | null;
   312тЖТ  }[];
   313тЖТ  
   314тЖТ  // PM material requirements (NEW)
   315тЖТ  pending_pms: {
   316тЖТ    pm_id: string;
   317тЖТ    train_id: string;
   318тЖТ    pm_type: string;
   319тЖТ    target_date: string;
   320тЖТ    required_materials: {
   321тЖТ      part_number: string;
   322тЖТ      quantity: number;
   323тЖТ      criticality: 'BLOCKING' | 'DEFERRABLE';
   324тЖТ    }[];
   325тЖТ  }[];
   326тЖТ}
   327тЖТ```

   388тЖТ// Metrics (to stderr, OpenTelemetry format)
   389тЖТinterface MetricsMessage {
   390тЖТ  _type: 'metrics';
   391тЖТ  timestamp: number;
   392тЖТ  metrics: {
   393тЖТ    'optimizer.solve_time_ms': number;
   394тЖТ    'optimizer.branches_explored': number;

   406тЖТ
   407тЖТ// CRITICAL: Error handling messages (addresses silent failure risk)
   408тЖТ// See Gap Analysis GAP-003 for rationale
   409тЖТinterface ErrorMessage {
   410тЖТ  type: 'ERROR';
   411тЖТ  request_id: string;
   412тЖТ  error_code: 'SEGFAULT' | 'TIMEOUT' | 'OOM' | 'INVALID_INPUT' | 'INTERNAL_ERROR';
   413тЖТ  error_message: string;
   414тЖТ  stack_trace?: string;  // When available
   415тЖТ  last_progress?: {
   416тЖТ    percent: number;
   417тЖТ    branches_explored: number;
   418тЖТ    memory_mb: number;
   419тЖТ  };
   420тЖТ  recovery_suggestion: string;  // Human-readable
   421тЖТ}
   422тЖТ
   423тЖТ// Heartbeat messages (sent every 5 seconds during solve)
   424тЖТinterface HeartbeatMessage {
   425тЖТ  type: 'HEARTBEAT';
   426тЖТ  request_id: string;
   427тЖТ  timestamp: number;
   428тЖТ  memory_mb: number;
   429тЖТ  thread_count: number;
   430тЖТ  branches_per_second: number;
   431тЖТ}
   432тЖТ```
   433тЖТ
   434тЖТ### 2.4.4 Error Handling Protocol
   435тЖТ
   436тЖТ**CRITICAL:** The C++ optimizer communicates via stdin/stdout streams. Stream failures (segfaults, hangs) must be detectable by the TypeScript worker.
   437тЖТ
   438тЖТ```typescript
   439тЖТ// Worker-side error handling (TypeScript)
   440тЖТclass OptimizerWorker {
   441тЖТ  private process: ChildProcess;
   442тЖТ  private heartbeatTimeout: NodeJS.Timeout | null = null;
   443тЖТ  private lastHeartbeat: number = 0;
   444тЖТ  
   445тЖТ  private readonly HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS = 5000;
   446тЖТ  private readonly HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT_MS = 15000;  // 3 missed heartbeats
   447тЖТ  
   448тЖТ  async solve(request: OptimizerInput): Promise<OptimizerOutput> {
   449тЖТ    return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
   450тЖТ      // Start heartbeat monitor
   451тЖТ      this.startHeartbeatMonitor(request.request_id, reject);
   452тЖТ      
   453тЖТ      // Handle stream events
   454тЖТ      this.process.stdout.on('data', (chunk) => {
   455тЖТ        for (const line of chunk.toString().split('\n')) {
   456тЖТ          if (!line.trim()) continue;
   457тЖТ          
   458тЖТ          try {
   459тЖТ            const msg = JSON.parse(line);
   460тЖТ            
   461тЖТ            switch (msg.type) {
   462тЖТ              case 'HEARTBEAT':
   463тЖТ                this.lastHeartbeat = Date.now();
   464тЖТ                break;
   465тЖТ                
   466тЖТ              case 'PROGRESS':
   467тЖТ                this.emitProgress(msg);
   468тЖТ                break;
   469тЖТ                
   470тЖТ              case 'RESULT':
   471тЖТ                this.stopHeartbeatMonitor();
   472тЖТ                resolve(msg);
   473тЖТ                break;
   474тЖТ                
   475тЖТ              case 'ERROR':
   476тЖТ                this.stopHeartbeatMonitor();
   477тЖТ                reject(new OptimizerError(msg));
   478тЖТ                break;
   479тЖТ            }
   480тЖТ          } catch (parseError) {
   481тЖТ            this.logger.warn({ line }, 'Failed to parse optimizer output');
   482тЖТ          }
   483тЖТ        }
   484тЖТ      });
   485тЖТ      
   486тЖТ      // Handle process crash
   487тЖТ      this.process.on('exit', (code, signal) => {
   488тЖТ        if (code !== 0) {
   489тЖТ          this.stopHeartbeatMonitor();
   490тЖТ          reject(new OptimizerError({
   491тЖТ            type: 'ERROR',
   492тЖТ            request_id: request.request_id,
   493тЖТ            error_code: signal === 'SIGSEGV' ? 'SEGFAULT' : 'INTERNAL_ERROR',
   494тЖТ            error_message: `Process exited with code ${code}, signal ${signal}`,
   495тЖТ            recovery_suggestion: 'Check optimizer logs and input data'
   496тЖТ          }));
   497тЖТ        }
   498тЖТ      });
   499тЖТ      
   500тЖТ      // Send request
   501тЖТ      this.process.stdin.write(JSON.stringify(request) + '\n');
   502тЖТ    });
   503тЖТ  }
   504тЖТ  
   505тЖТ  private startHeartbeatMonitor(requestId: string, reject: Function): void {
   506тЖТ    this.lastHeartbeat = Date.now();
   507тЖТ    
   508тЖТ    this.heartbeatTimeout = setInterval(() => {
   509тЖТ      const elapsed = Date.now() - this.lastHeartbeat;
   510тЖТ      
   511тЖТ      if (elapsed > this.HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT_MS) {
   512тЖТ        this.stopHeartbeatMonitor();
   513тЖТ        
   514тЖТ        // Kill hung process
   515тЖТ        this.process.kill('SIGKILL');
   516тЖТ        
   517тЖТ        reject(new OptimizerError({
   518тЖТ          type: 'ERROR',
   519тЖТ          request_id: requestId,
   520тЖТ          error_code: 'TIMEOUT',
   521тЖТ          error_message: `No heartbeat received for ${elapsed}ms`,
   522тЖТ          recovery_suggestion: 'Optimizer may be stuck. Consider reducing problem size.'
   523тЖТ        }));
   524тЖТ      }
   525тЖТ    }, this.HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS);
   526тЖТ  }
   527тЖТ  
   528тЖТ  private stopHeartbeatMonitor(): void {
   529тЖТ    if (this.heartbeatTimeout) {
   530тЖТ      clearInterval(this.heartbeatTimeout);
   531тЖТ      this.heartbeatTimeout = null;
   532тЖТ    }
   533тЖТ  }
   534тЖТ}
   535тЖТ
   536тЖТclass OptimizerError extends Error {
   537тЖТ  readonly errorCode: string;
   538тЖТ  readonly requestId: string;
   539тЖТ  readonly recoverySuggestion: string;
   540тЖТ  readonly stackTrace?: string;
   541тЖТ  
   542тЖТ  constructor(msg: ErrorMessage) {
   543тЖТ    super(msg.error_message);
   544тЖТ    this.name = 'OptimizerError';
   545тЖТ    this.errorCode = msg.error_code;
   546тЖТ    this.requestId = msg.request_id;
   547тЖТ    this.recoverySuggestion = msg.recovery_suggestion;
   548тЖТ    this.stackTrace = msg.stack_trace;
   549тЖТ  }
   550тЖТ}
   551тЖТ```
   552тЖТ
   553тЖТ**C++ Implementation Notes:**
   554тЖТ
   555тЖТThe C++ binary must:
   556тЖТ1. Install signal handlers for SIGSEGV, SIGABRT, SIGFPE
   557тЖТ2. Send heartbeat messages every 5 seconds during solve
   558тЖТ3. Catch exceptions and emit ERROR messages before exit
   559тЖТ4. Flush stdout after every message (line-buffered)
   560тЖТ
   561тЖТ```cpp
   562тЖТ// Signal handler example (C++)
   563тЖТvoid signalHandler(int signal) {
   564тЖТ    nlohmann::json error;
   565тЖТ    error["type"] = "ERROR";
   566тЖТ    error["request_id"] = currentRequestId;
   567тЖТ    error["error_code"] = (signal == SIGSEGV) ? "SEGFAULT" : "INTERNAL_ERROR";
   568тЖТ    error["error_message"] = strsignal(signal);
   569тЖТ    error["stack_trace"] = captureStackTrace();
   570тЖТ    error["recovery_suggestion"] = "Internal optimizer error. Check input data.";
   571тЖТ    
   572тЖТ    std::cout << error.dump() << std::endl;
   573тЖТ    std::cout.flush();
   574тЖТ    
   575тЖТ    _exit(128 + signal);
   576тЖТ}
   577тЖТ```
   578тЖТ
   579тЖТThe Constraint Aggregation Layer ensures consistency between Strategic and Tactical planning by computing capacity envelopes.
   580тЖТ
   581тЖТ### 2.5.1 Purpose
   582тЖТ
   583тЖТThe Strategic Planner operates at quarterly granularity and cannot evaluate detailed daily constraints. However, it must not create plans that the Tactical Scheduler will reject. The Constraint Aggregation Layer bridges this gap.
   584тЖТ
   585тЖТ### 2.5.2 Aggregation Process
   586тЖТ
   587тЖТ```typescript
   588тЖТinterface ConstraintEnvelope {
   589тЖТ  period: {
   590тЖТ    start: Date;  // Quarter start
   591тЖТ    end: Date;    // Quarter end
   592тЖТ  };
   593тЖТ  
   594тЖТ  // Track capacity (aggregated from daily track availability)
   595тЖТ  track_capacity: {
   596тЖТ    depot_id: string;
   597тЖТ    track_type: 'LIGHT' | 'HEAVY' | 'OVERHAUL';
   598тЖТ    total_slot_days: number;      // Sum of available days in quarter
   599тЖТ    reserved_slot_days: number;   // Already committed
   600тЖТ    available_slot_days: number;  // Remaining capacity
   601тЖТ  }[];
   602тЖТ  
   603тЖТ  // Labor capacity (aggregated from weekly man-hour projections)
   604тЖТ  labor_capacity: {
   605тЖТ    depot_id: string;
   606тЖТ    regular_hours: number;        // Total regular hours in quarter
   607тЖТ    overtime_hours: number;       // Total overtime available
   608тЖТ    committed_hours: number;      // Already scheduled
   609тЖТ    available_hours: number;      // Remaining capacity
   610тЖТ  }[];
   611тЖТ  
   612тЖТ  // Material capacity (aggregated from inventory projections)
   613тЖТ  material_capacity: {
   614тЖТ    depot_id: string;
   615тЖТ    part_category: string;        // e.g., 'BOGIE_KIT', 'BRAKE_SET'
   616тЖТ    expected_available: number;   // Projected inventory
   617тЖТ    committed: number;            // Reserved for scheduled PMs
   618тЖТ    available: number;            // Remaining for new scheduling
   619тЖТ  }[];
   620тЖТ  
   621тЖТ  // Constraints from tactical model
   622тЖТ  blocked_trains: {
   623тЖТ    train_id: string;
   624тЖТ    blocked_weeks: number;        // Weeks unavailable in quarter
   625тЖТ    reason: string;
   626тЖТ  }[];
   627тЖТ}
   628тЖТ
   629тЖТclass ConstraintAggregationService {
   630тЖТ  /**
   631тЖТ   * Generate constraint envelope for strategic planning
   632тЖТ   */
   633тЖТ  async generateEnvelope(
   634тЖТ    quarterStart: Date,
   635тЖТ    quarterEnd: Date,
   636тЖТ    depotIds: string[]
   637тЖТ  ): Promise<ConstraintEnvelope> {
   638тЖТ    // 1. Aggregate track availability
   639тЖТ    const trackCapacity = await this.aggregateTrackCapacity(
   640тЖТ      quarterStart, quarterEnd, depotIds
   641тЖТ    );
   642тЖТ    
   643тЖТ    // 2. Aggregate labor capacity
   644тЖТ    const laborCapacity = await this.aggregateLaborCapacity(
   645тЖТ      quarterStart, quarterEnd, depotIds
   646тЖТ    );
   647тЖТ    
   648тЖТ    // 3. Aggregate material projections
   649тЖТ    const materialCapacity = await this.aggregateMaterialCapacity(
   650тЖТ      quarterStart, quarterEnd, depotIds
   651тЖТ    );
   652тЖТ    
   653тЖТ    // 4. Identify blocked trains from tactical constraints
   654тЖТ    const blockedTrains = await this.identifyBlockedTrains(
   655тЖТ      quarterStart, quarterEnd
   656тЖТ    );
   657тЖТ    
   658тЖТ    return {
   659тЖТ      period: { start: quarterStart, end: quarterEnd },
   660тЖТ      track_capacity: trackCapacity,
   661тЖТ      labor_capacity: laborCapacity,
   662тЖТ      material_capacity: materialCapacity,
   663тЖТ      blocked_trains: blockedTrains
   664тЖТ    };
   665тЖТ  }
   666тЖТ  
   667тЖТ  /**
   668тЖТ   * Validate strategic allocation against constraint envelope
   669тЖТ   */
   670тЖТ  async validateStrategicAllocation(
   671тЖТ    allocation: StrategicAllocation,
   672тЖТ    envelope: ConstraintEnvelope
   673тЖТ  ): Promise<ValidationResult> {
   674тЖТ    const violations: ConstraintViolation[] = [];
   675тЖТ    
   676тЖТ    // Check track capacity
   677тЖТ    for (const trackReq of allocation.track_requirements) {
   678тЖТ      const available = envelope.track_capacity.find(
   679тЖТ        t => t.depot_id === trackReq.depot_id && 
   680тЖТ             t.track_type === trackReq.track_type
   681тЖТ      );
   682тЖТ      
   683тЖТ      if (available && trackReq.slot_days > available.available_slot_days) {
   684тЖТ        violations.push({
   685тЖТ          type: 'TRACK_OVERCAPACITY',
   686тЖТ          severity: 'HARD',
   687тЖТ          message: `${trackReq.track_type} track at ${trackReq.depot_id} overbooked by ${trackReq.slot_days - available.available_slot_days} days`
   688тЖТ        });
   689тЖТ      }
   690тЖТ    }
   691тЖТ    
   692тЖТ    // Check labor capacity
   693тЖТ    // Check material capacity
   694тЖТ    // ... similar validation logic
   695тЖТ    
   696тЖТ    return {
   697тЖТ      valid: violations.filter(v => v.severity === 'HARD').length === 0,
   698тЖТ      violations
   699тЖТ    };
   700тЖТ  }
   701тЖТ}
   702тЖТ```
   703тЖТ
   704тЖТ---
   705тЖТ
   706тЖТ## 2.6 Key Design Principles
   707тЖТ
   708тЖТ### 2.6.1 Explainability First
   709тЖТ
   710тЖТEvery optimization decision must be traceable to specific constraints:
   711тЖТ
   712тЖТ- **Run assignments**: Include explanation factors in API response
   713тЖТ- **PM scheduling**: Log which constraints drove date selection
   714тЖТ- **Man-hour adjustments**: Record rationale for each proposed change
   715тЖТ- **Strategic allocations**: Document constraint envelope used
   716тЖТ
   717тЖТ### 2.6.2 Graceful Degradation
   718тЖТ
   719тЖТThe system must provide useful output even when optimal solutions are impossible:
   720тЖТ
   721тЖТ- **C++ solver timeout**: Fall back to TypeScript greedy solver
   722тЖТ- **Material unavailability**: Flag but continue with labor optimization
   723тЖТ- **Constraint conflicts**: Return least-bad solution with violation list
   724тЖТ- **Data quality issues**: Proceed with validated subset, quarantine bad records
   725тЖТ
   726тЖТ### 2.6.3 Audit Everything
   727тЖТ
   728тЖТFor 12-year government contract compliance:
   729тЖТ
   730тЖТ- **All schedule changes**: Who, when, what, why
   731тЖТ- **All baseline publications**: Snapshot contents, approval chain
   732тЖТ- **All scenario promotions**: Delta applied, approval
   733тЖТ- **All system decisions**: Optimizer inputs/outputs preserved
   734тЖТ
   735тЖТ### 2.6.4 Stable Long-Term Planning
   736тЖТ
   737тЖТPrevent "jittery" forecasts that erode planner trust:
   738тЖТ
   739тЖТ- **Published baselines**: Immutable reference points
   740тЖТ- **Drift thresholds**: Only re-plan when variance exceeds 5%
   741тЖТ- **Dampening**: Strategic forecasts use rolling averages, not daily deltas
   742тЖТ- **Explicit triggers**: Users control when strategic re-planning occurs
   743тЖТ

Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

## 2.2 Component Responsibilities

### 2.2.1 Frontend (Infinite Canvas SPA)

| Responsibility | Implementation |
|----------------|----------------|
| Timeline rendering | deck.gl WebGL layers with semantic zoom |
| Strategic view | Heatmap showing PM density per train per year |
| Tactical view | Gantt blocks for 12-week PM schedule |
| Operational view | Detail cards with technician, track, duration |
| Baseline/drift overlay | Shadow layer showing deviation from published plan |
| Constraint walls | Visual barriers at compliance window boundaries |
| Drag-and-drop rescheduling | Real-time validation with constraint feedback |
| Man-hour visualization | Column overlay showing utilization with/without adjustments |
| Scenario comparison | Side-by-side diff view for what-if analysis |
| Real-time collaboration | WebSocket sync for multi-user canvas editing |

### 2.2.2 Backend API (Bun + Hono)

| Responsibility | Implementation |
|----------------|----------------|
| REST API | `/api/v1/*` endpoints with OpenAPI spec |
| WebSocket hub | Real-time canvas updates and collaboration |
| Authentication | JWT with refresh tokens, RBAC enforcement |
| Business logic | Orchestration layer for domain services |
| Task dispatch | BullMQ job queuing for async operations |
| Optimizer IPC | Stream-based communication with C++ binary |
| Audit logging | Immutable change records with user attribution |

### 2.2.3 File Watcher Service

| Responsibility | Implementation |
|----------------|----------------|
| Folder monitoring | chokidar watching `/data/inbox/` |
| File validation | Format, size, checksum verification |
| Queue dispatch | BullMQ job creation for ingestion worker |
| Archive management | Move processed files to `/data/processed/` |
| Quarantine handling | Move invalid files to `/data/quarantine/` |
| Health alerting | Notification when files stop arriving (>2 hours) |

### 2.2.4 BullMQ Workers

| Worker | Responsibility | Concurrency |
|--------|----------------|-------------|
| `ingestion_worker` | Parse CSV, validate rows, insert to DB, handle meter swaps | 2 |
| `optimizer_worker` | Spawn C++ binary, stream I/O, store results with explanations | 1 per depot |
| `tactical_scheduler` | 12-week PM scheduling with material checks | 1 |
| `strategic_planner` | 1-10 year capacity reservation with constraint envelope | 1 |
| `manhour_leveling_worker` | Weekly/monthly workload balancing, adjustment generation | 1 |
| `drift_monitor_worker` | Nightly baseline comparison, threshold alerting | 1 |
| `report_worker` | Generate PDF/Excel reports | 2 |
| `notification_worker` | Send email alerts and notifications | 3 |

### 2.2.5 C++ Optimizer Binary

| Responsibility | Implementation |
|----------------|----------------|
| Constraint solving | OR-Tools CP-SAT with soft constraint penalties |
| Explanation generation | Factor-based trace for each assignment decision |
| Progress streaming | NDJSON messages to stdout during solve |
| Metrics emission | OpenTelemetry format to stderr |
| Fallback mode | Greedy heuristic when CP-SAT times out |
| Material validation | Check inventory positions for blocking parts |

## 2.3 Critical Data Flows

### 2.3.1 Daily Operational Flow

```
06:00 тФВ CSV file drop (mileage.csv) тЖТ File Watcher detects
06:05 тФВ Ingestion Worker: Parse, validate, insert mileage readings
06:10 тФВ Ingestion Worker: Update train current_mileage, detect anomalies
06:15 тФВ Drift Monitor: Check against baseline, flag significant deviations
06:30 тФВ Optimizer Worker: Generate today's run assignments
      тФВ   - Input: trains, routes, constraints, inventory positions
      тФВ   - Output: assignments with explanation traces
07:00 тФВ WebSocket: Push results to connected canvas clients
07:00 тФВ Operators: Review assignments on Infinite Canvas
      тФВ   - View explanation factors for each assignment
      тФВ   - Drag-and-drop to override if needed (with audit trail)
08:00 тФВ Export: Approved assignments sent to OCC system
16:00 тФВ Actual mileage updates (if available intra-day)
22:00 тФВ Optimizer Worker: Generate tomorrow's assignments
```

### 2.3.2 Weekly Planning Flow

```
Monday 06:00  тФВ Strategic Planner: Run 10-year horizon update
              тФВ   - Uses constraint envelope from tactical model
              тФВ   - Respects published baseline (drift threshold)
Monday 06:30  тФВ Tactical Scheduler: Run 12-week PM scheduling
              тФВ   - Check material availability for T+14 days (hard)
              тФВ   - Check material availability for T+90 days (soft)
Monday 07:00  тФВ Man-hour Leveling: Generate 12-week projection
              тФВ   - Identify overloaded/underutilized weeks
              тФВ   - Propose adjustments (defer/advance/overtime)
Monday 08:00  тФВ WebSocket: Push updated canvas data to all clients
Tuesday       тФВ Planners: Review PM conflicts on canvas
              тФВ   - Drag to resolve conflicts (audit trail)
              тФВ   - Review/approve man-hour adjustments
Wednesday    тФВ Report Worker: Generate resource utilization report
Friday       тФВ Notification Worker: Send weekly compliance report
```

### 2.3.3 Baseline Publishing Flow

```
User Action  тФВ Planner clicks "Publish Baseline" on canvas
             тФВ   - System validates: no critical conflicts
             тФВ   - System prompts: "This will create Baseline v3.2"
API Call     тФВ POST /api/v1/baselines
             тФВ   - Snapshot current pm_schedule to baseline_pm_schedule
             тФВ   - Snapshot mileage projections to baseline_mileage_projection
             тФВ   - Set previous baseline status to SUPERSEDED
             тФВ   - Set new baseline status to PUBLISHED
Post-Publish тФВ Drift Monitor: Now compares against new baseline
             тФВ Canvas: Shows drift layer relative to new baseline
```

### 2.3.4 Scenario Comparison Flow

```
User Action  тФВ Planner clicks "Create Scenario" on canvas
             тФВ   - System forks from current published baseline
             тФВ   - New scenario_id assigned
Editing      тФВ Planner makes changes within scenario
             тФВ   - All changes stored as deltas (event-sourced)
             тФВ   - Original baseline data unchanged
Comparison   тФВ Planner clicks "Compare Scenarios"
             тФВ   - System materializes both scenarios
             тФВ   - Canvas shows side-by-side diff view
             тФВ   - Metrics: cost impact, resource utilization delta
Promotion    тФВ Planner clicks "Promote to Baseline"
             тФВ   - Deltas applied to create new baseline
             тФВ   - Scenario marked as PROMOTED
```

### 2.4.2 Input Message Schema

```typescript
interface OptimizerInput {
  type: 'SOLVE_REQUEST';
  request_id: string;  // UUID for correlation
  config: {
    max_solve_time_seconds: number;  // Default: 30
    num_workers: number;             // Default: 4
    soft_constraint_penalties: {
      mileage_imbalance: number;     // Default: 100
      preference_violation: number;  // Default: 50
      overtime_hours: number;        // Default: 200
    };
    explanation_verbosity: 'MINIMAL' | 'STANDARD' | 'DETAILED';
  };
  
  trains: {
    id: string;
    train_number: string;
    fleet_id: string;
    current_mileage: number;
    target_mileage: number;  // For balancing calculation
    fitness_status: 'FIT' | 'RESTRICTED' | 'GROUNDED';
    restrictions: string[];  // e.g., ['NO_EXPRESS', 'MAX_SPEED_60']
    home_depot_id: string;
    current_depot_id: string;
  }[];
  
  routes: {
    id: string;
    route_code: string;
    expected_km: number;
    required_trains: number;
    allowed_fleets: string[];
    restrictions: string[];  // e.g., ['REQUIRES_ATO', 'LONG_PLATFORM']
  }[];
  
  // Hard constraints (must not violate)
  hard_constraints: {
    type: 'PM_DUE' | 'GROUNDED' | 'DEPOT_LOCK' | 'CERTIFICATION';
    train_id: string;
    description: string;
  }[];
  
  // Soft constraints (minimize violations)
  soft_constraints: {
    type: 'MILEAGE_BALANCE' | 'DEPOT_PREFERENCE' | 'ROUTE_PREFERENCE';
    train_id: string;
    penalty_weight: number;
    description: string;
  }[];
  
  // Material availability (NEW)
  material_positions: {
    depot_id: string;
    part_number: string;
    quantity_available: number;  // on_hand - reserved
    next_delivery_date: string | null;
  }[];
  
  // PM material requirements (NEW)
  pending_pms: {
    pm_id: string;
    train_id: string;
    pm_type: string;
    target_date: string;
    required_materials: {
      part_number: string;
      quantity: number;
      criticality: 'BLOCKING' | 'DEFERRABLE';
    }[];
  }[];
}
```

}

// CRITICAL: Error handling messages (addresses silent failure risk)
// See Gap Analysis GAP-003 for rationale
interface ErrorMessage {
  type: 'ERROR';
  request_id: string;
  error_code: 'SEGFAULT' | 'TIMEOUT' | 'OOM' | 'INVALID_INPUT' | 'INTERNAL_ERROR';
  error_message: string;
  stack_trace?: string;  // When available
  last_progress?: {
    percent: number;
    branches_explored: number;
    memory_mb: number;
  };

}

// Heartbeat messages (sent every 5 seconds during solve)
interface HeartbeatMessage {
  type: 'HEARTBEAT';
  request_id: string;
  timestamp: number;
  memory_mb: number;
  thread_count: number;
  branches_per_second: number;
}
```

### 2.4.4 Error Handling Protocol

**CRITICAL:** The C++ optimizer communicates via stdin/stdout streams. Stream failures (segfaults, hangs) must be detectable by the TypeScript worker.

```typescript
// Worker-side error handling (TypeScript)
class OptimizerWorker {
  private process: ChildProcess;
  private heartbeatTimeout: NodeJS.Timeout | null = null;
  private lastHeartbeat: number = 0;
  
  private readonly HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS = 5000;
  private readonly HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT_MS = 15000;  // 3 missed heartbeats
  
  async solve(request: OptimizerInput): Promise<OptimizerOutput> {
    return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
      // Start heartbeat monitor
      this.startHeartbeatMonitor(request.request_id, reject);
      
      // Handle stream events
      this.process.stdout.on('data', (chunk) => {
        for (const line of chunk.toString().split('\n')) {
          if (!line.trim()) continue;
          
          try {
            const msg = JSON.parse(line);
            
            switch (msg.type) {
              case 'HEARTBEAT':
                this.lastHeartbeat = Date.now();
                break;
                
              case 'PROGRESS':
                this.emitProgress(msg);
                break;
                
              case 'RESULT':
                this.stopHeartbeatMonitor();
                resolve(msg);
                break;
                
              case 'ERROR':
                this.stopHeartbeatMonitor();
                reject(new OptimizerError(msg));
                break;
            }
          } catch (parseError) {
            this.logger.warn({ line }, 'Failed to parse optimizer output');
          }
        }
      });
      
      // Handle process crash
      this.process.on('exit', (code, signal) => {
        if (code !== 0) {
          this.stopHeartbeatMonitor();
          reject(new OptimizerError({
            type: 'ERROR',
            request_id: request.request_id,
            error_code: signal === 'SIGSEGV' ? 'SEGFAULT' : 'INTERNAL_ERROR',
            error_message: `Process exited with code ${code}, signal ${signal}`,
            recovery_suggestion: 'Check optimizer logs and input data'
          }));
        }
      });
      
      // Send request
      this.process.stdin.write(JSON.stringify(request) + '\n');
    });
  }
  
  private startHeartbeatMonitor(requestId: string, reject: Function): void {
    this.lastHeartbeat = Date.now();
    
    this.heartbeatTimeout = setInterval(() => {
      const elapsed = Date.now() - this.lastHeartbeat;
      
      if (elapsed > this.HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT_MS) {
        this.stopHeartbeatMonitor();
        
        // Kill hung process
        this.process.kill('SIGKILL');
        
        reject(new OptimizerError({
          type: 'ERROR',
          request_id: requestId,
          error_code: 'TIMEOUT',
          error_message: `No heartbeat received for ${elapsed}ms`,
          recovery_suggestion: 'Optimizer may be stuck. Consider reducing problem size.'
        }));
      }
    }, this.HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS);
  }
  
  private stopHeartbeatMonitor(): void {
    if (this.heartbeatTimeout) {
      clearInterval(this.heartbeatTimeout);
      this.heartbeatTimeout = null;
    }
  }
}

class OptimizerError extends Error {
  readonly errorCode: string;
  readonly requestId: string;
  readonly recoverySuggestion: string;
  readonly stackTrace?: string;
  
  constructor(msg: ErrorMessage) {
    super(msg.error_message);
    this.name = 'OptimizerError';
    this.errorCode = msg.error_code;
    this.requestId = msg.request_id;
    this.recoverySuggestion = msg.recovery_suggestion;
    this.stackTrace = msg.stack_trace;
  }
}
```

**C++ Implementation Notes:**

The C++ binary must:
1. Install signal handlers for SIGSEGV, SIGABRT, SIGFPE
2. Send heartbeat messages every 5 seconds during solve
3. Catch exceptions and emit ERROR messages before exit
4. Flush stdout after every message (line-buffered)

```cpp
// Signal handler example (C++)
void signalHandler(int signal) {
    nlohmann::json error;
    error["type"] = "ERROR";
    error["request_id"] = currentRequestId;
    error["error_code"] = (signal == SIGSEGV) ? "SEGFAULT" : "INTERNAL_ERROR";
    error["error_message"] = strsignal(signal);
    error["stack_trace"] = captureStackTrace();
    error["recovery_suggestion"] = "Internal optimizer error. Check input data.";
    
    std::cout << error.dump() << std::endl;
    std::cout.flush();
    
    _exit(128 + signal);
}
```

The Constraint Aggregation Layer ensures consistency between Strategic and Tactical planning by computing capacity envelopes.

### 2.5.1 Purpose

The Strategic Planner operates at quarterly granularity and cannot evaluate detailed daily constraints. However, it must not create plans that the Tactical Scheduler will reject. The Constraint Aggregation Layer bridges this gap.

### 2.5.2 Aggregation Process

```typescript
interface ConstraintEnvelope {
  period: {
    start: Date;  // Quarter start
    end: Date;    // Quarter end
  };
  
  // Track capacity (aggregated from daily track availability)
  track_capacity: {
    depot_id: string;
    track_type: 'LIGHT' | 'HEAVY' | 'OVERHAUL';
    total_slot_days: number;      // Sum of available days in quarter
    reserved_slot_days: number;   // Already committed
    available_slot_days: number;  // Remaining capacity
  }[];
  
  // Labor capacity (aggregated from weekly man-hour projections)
  labor_capacity: {
    depot_id: string;
    regular_hours: number;        // Total regular hours in quarter
    overtime_hours: number;       // Total overtime available
    committed_hours: number;      // Already scheduled
    available_hours: number;      // Remaining capacity
  }[];
  
  // Material capacity (aggregated from inventory projections)
  material_capacity: {
    depot_id: string;
    part_category: string;        // e.g., 'BOGIE_KIT', 'BRAKE_SET'
    expected_available: number;   // Projected inventory
    committed: number;            // Reserved for scheduled PMs
    available: number;            // Remaining for new scheduling
  }[];
  
  // Constraints from tactical model
  blocked_trains: {
    train_id: string;
    blocked_weeks: number;        // Weeks unavailable in quarter
    reason: string;
  }[];
}

class ConstraintAggregationService {
  /**
   * Generate constraint envelope for strategic planning
   */
  async generateEnvelope(
    quarterStart: Date,
    quarterEnd: Date,
    depotIds: string[]
  ): Promise<ConstraintEnvelope> {
    // 1. Aggregate track availability
    const trackCapacity = await this.aggregateTrackCapacity(
      quarterStart, quarterEnd, depotIds
    );
    
    // 2. Aggregate labor capacity
    const laborCapacity = await this.aggregateLaborCapacity(
      quarterStart, quarterEnd, depotIds
    );
    
    // 3. Aggregate material projections
    const materialCapacity = await this.aggregateMaterialCapacity(
      quarterStart, quarterEnd, depotIds
    );
    
    // 4. Identify blocked trains from tactical constraints
    const blockedTrains = await this.identifyBlockedTrains(
      quarterStart, quarterEnd
    );
    
    return {
      period: { start: quarterStart, end: quarterEnd },
      track_capacity: trackCapacity,
      labor_capacity: laborCapacity,
      material_capacity: materialCapacity,
      blocked_trains: blockedTrains
    };
  }
  
  /**
   * Validate strategic allocation against constraint envelope
   */
  async validateStrategicAllocation(
    allocation: StrategicAllocation,
    envelope: ConstraintEnvelope
  ): Promise<ValidationResult> {
    const violations: ConstraintViolation[] = [];
    
    // Check track capacity
    for (const trackReq of allocation.track_requirements) {
      const available = envelope.track_capacity.find(
        t => t.depot_id === trackReq.depot_id && 
             t.track_type === trackReq.track_type
      );
      
      if (available && trackReq.slot_days > available.available_slot_days) {
        violations.push({
          type: 'TRACK_OVERCAPACITY',
          severity: 'HARD',
          message: `${trackReq.track_type} track at ${trackReq.depot_id} overbooked by ${trackReq.slot_days - available.available_slot_days} days`
        });
      }
    }
    
    // Check labor capacity
    // Check material capacity
    // ... similar validation logic
    
    return {
      valid: violations.filter(v => v.severity === 'HARD').length === 0,
      violations
    };
  }
}
```

---

## 2.6 Key Design Principles

### 2.6.1 Explainability First

Every optimization decision must be traceable to specific constraints:

- **Run assignments**: Include explanation factors in API response
- **PM scheduling**: Log which constraints drove date selection
- **Man-hour adjustments**: Record rationale for each proposed change
- **Strategic allocations**: Document constraint envelope used

### 2.6.2 Graceful Degradation

The system must provide useful output even when optimal solutions are impossible:

- **C++ solver timeout**: Fall back to TypeScript greedy solver
- **Material unavailability**: Flag but continue with labor optimization
- **Constraint conflicts**: Return least-bad solution with violation list
- **Data quality issues**: Proceed with validated subset, quarantine bad records

### 2.6.3 Audit Everything

For 12-year government contract compliance:

- **All schedule changes**: Who, when, what, why
- **All baseline publications**: Snapshot contents, approval chain
- **All scenario promotions**: Delta applied, approval
- **All system decisions**: Optimizer inputs/outputs preserved

### 2.6.4 Stable Long-Term Planning

Prevent "jittery" forecasts that erode planner trust:

- **Published baselines**: Immutable reference points
- **Drift thresholds**: Only re-plan when variance exceeds 5%
- **Dampening**: Strategic forecasts use rolling averages, not daily deltas
- **Explicit triggers**: Users control when strategic re-planning occurs

# Metro Fleet Mileage Management System
# Part 6: User Interface Specification

**Document Version:** 2.0  
**Status:** APPROVED FOR DEVELOPMENT

---

## 6.1 Design System Overview

### 6.1.1 Technology Stack

| Component | Technology | Purpose |
|-----------|------------|---------|
| Framework | React 18 | Component architecture |
| State | Zustand | Global state management |
| Canvas | deck.gl | WebGL timeline rendering |
| Components | BlueprintJS v6 | UI component library |
| Styling | Tailwind CSS | Utility-first styling |
| Build | Vite | Development and bundling |
| Types | TypeScript | Type safety |

### 6.1.2 Design Principles

1. **Information Density**: Maximize data visibility without overwhelming
2. **Semantic Zoom**: Data representation adapts to zoom level
3. **Direct Manipulation**: Drag-and-drop with immediate feedback
4. **Progressive Disclosure**: Details on demand
5. **Constraint Visibility**: Show limits, not just violations
6. **Explainability**: Every system decision has visible rationale

### 6.1.3 Color System

```typescript
const colors = {
  // PM Status
  pm: {
    scheduled: '#3B82F6',    // Blue
    inProgress: '#F59E0B',   // Amber
    completed: '#10B981',    // Green
    cancelled: '#6B7280',    // Gray
    deferred: '#8B5CF6',     // Purple
    overdue: '#EF4444',      // Red
  },
  
  // Constraint Indicators
  constraints: {
    hardViolation: '#DC2626',    // Red-600
    softViolation: '#F59E0B',    // Amber-500
    approaching: '#FBBF24',      // Yellow-400
    ok: '#10B981',               // Green-500
  },
  
  // Drift Severity
  drift: {
    critical: '#DC2626',     // Red
    warning: '#F59E0B',      // Amber
    ok: '#10B981',           // Green
  },
  
  // Utilization
  utilization: {
    overloaded: '#DC2626',   // Red
    optimal: '#10B981',      // Green
    underutilized: '#3B82F6', // Blue
  },
  
  // Fleet Colors
  fleets: {
    KS: '#0EA5E9',           // Sky-500
    ALSTOM: '#8B5CF6',       // Violet-500
  }
};
```

---

    13тЖТ### 9.1.1 Strategic Goals
    14тЖТ
    15тЖТ| Goal | How Demo Achieves It |
    16тЖТ|------|---------------------|
    17тЖТ| **Immediate "wow"** | Semantic zoom from 12-year to daily in one gesture |
    18тЖТ| **Solve Excel pain** | Visualize entire fleet timeline (impossible in spreadsheets) |
    19тЖТ| **Differentiate** | Competitors show paginated tables; we show infinite canvas |
    20тЖТ| **Prove depth** | WebGL rendering at scale signals engineering capability |
    21тЖТ| **Use their data** | Import their actual fleet, show their actual bow-wave |

# Metro Fleet Mileage Management System
# Part 9: Demo Application Specification

**Document Version:** 1.0  
**Status:** APPROVED FOR DEMO DEVELOPMENT  
**Purpose:** Win-the-bid client demonstration  
**Target:** Single-user utility with live data import

---

## 9.1 Demo Objectives

### 9.1.1 Strategic Goals

| Goal | How Demo Achieves It |
|------|---------------------|
| **Immediate "wow"** | Semantic zoom from 12-year to daily in one gesture |
| **Solve Excel pain** | Visualize entire fleet timeline (impossible in spreadsheets) |
| **Differentiate** | Competitors show paginated tables; we show infinite canvas |
| **Prove depth** | WebGL rendering at scale signals engineering capability |
| **Use their data** | Import their actual fleet, show their actual bow-wave |

### 9.1.2 Demo Script (The Pitch)

**The "Financial Impact" Hook Version:**

```
1. INGEST (30 sec)
   "I'm dropping in your raw Maximo export."
   ├втАатАЩ Drag CSV, watch trains appear instantly

2. THE REVEAL - Strategic View (60 sec)
   "This is the 10-year view."
   ├втАатАЩ Zoom out to heatmap
   "See this red cluster in Q2 2027? That's a 'Bow Wave'."
   "You have 40% of your fleet due for C4 overhauls simultaneously."
   "That's a $15M capital risk in a single quarter."

3. THE ZOOM - Tactical View (45 sec)
   "Let's zoom in."
   ├втАатАЩ Smooth zoom to Gantt
   "Here are the specific trains causing the jam."
   ├втАатАЩ Point at 3-4 overlapping C4 blocks

4. THE SOLVE - Operational View (60 sec)
   "Watch this."
   ├втАатАЩ Drag a C4 block left
   "I move this C4 three months earlier."
   ├втАатАЩ Green snap, constraint walls flash
   "The system instantly checks parts, labor, and track availability."
   ├втАатАЩ Toggle man-hour overlay
   "This week was 120% capacity. Now it's 85%."

5. THE CLOSE (30 sec)
   ├втАатАЩ Zoom back out
   "We just smoothed a $15M peak in 3 seconds."
   "One interface. One truth. 12 years."
   "Maximo can't do that. Excel can't do that."
```

**If Client Data Shows No Bow-Wave:**

Use the Chaos Monkey feature (Section 9.3.3):
```
"Your data actually looks healthy - that's good news."
"But let me show you what happens with a 3-month supply chain disruption..."
├втАатАЩ Toggle Chaos Monkey
"See how that wave forms in 2027? This is why you need visibility."
```

### 9.1.3 Scope Boundaries

**IN SCOPE:**
- CSV import (fleet roster, PM history)
- PM projection engine (10-year horizon)
- Infinite canvas with semantic zoom (3 levels minimum)
- Drag-and-drop with constraint validation
- Mileage balance visualization
- Man-hour overlay (simulated capacity)
- Bow-wave indicator
- Chaos Monkey toggle (hidden, for demo resilience)

**OUT OF SCOPE:**
- Backend/database (use in-memory)
- Authentication
- Real optimizer (use projection heuristics)
- Maximo integration
- Multi-user collaboration
- Report generation
- Component-level tracking

---

## 9.2 Data Model

### 9.2.1 CSV Input Schemas

#### CSV 1: Fleet Roster (REQUIRED)

```csv

    47тЖТ## 7.2 Integration Phasing
    48тЖТ
    49тЖТ| Phase | Timeline | Integrations | Method |
    50тЖТ|-------|----------|--------------|--------|
    51тЖТ| **Phase 1** | Months 1-6 | Mileage CSV Import | File drop |
    52тЖТ| | | Run Assignment Export | CSV export |
    53тЖТ| | | Email Notifications | SMTP |
    54тЖТ| **Phase 2** | Months 7-12 | Maximo Read | REST API |
    55тЖТ| | | Inventory Snapshot | CSV/API |
    56тЖТ| | | Cost Data Import | CSV |
    57тЖТ| **Phase 3** | Months 13-18 | Maximo Bidirectional | REST API + Webhooks |
    58тЖТ| | | OCC Integration | Real-time API |
    59тЖТ| | | ERP Integration | REST API |
    60тЖТ
    61тЖТ---
    62тЖТ
    63тЖТ## 7.3 Mileage Data Ingestion
    64тЖТ
    65тЖТ### 7.3.1 CSV File Format
    66тЖТ
    67тЖТ**Filename Pattern:** `mileage_YYYYMMDD.csv`  
    68тЖТ**Encoding:** UTF-8  
    69тЖТ**Delimiter:** Comma  
    70тЖТ**Header:** Required
    71тЖТ
    72тЖТ```csv
    73тЖТtrain_number,reading_date,odometer_km,source
    74тЖТ1001,2026-01-14,245152,ATS
    75тЖТ1002,2026-01-14,248523,ATS
    76тЖТ1003,2026-01-14,242087,ATS
    77тЖТ```
    78тЖТ
    79тЖТ### 7.3.2 Field Specifications
    80тЖТ
    81тЖТ| Field | Type | Required | Validation |
    82тЖТ|-------|------|----------|------------|
    83тЖТ| `train_number` | string | Yes | Must exist in trains table |
    84тЖТ| `reading_date` | date | Yes | Format: YYYY-MM-DD, not future |
    85тЖТ| `odometer_km` | integer | Yes | Positive, тЙд previous + 2000 |
    86тЖТ| `source` | string | No | Default: 'CSV' |
    87тЖТ
    88тЖТ### 7.3.3 Validation Rules
    89тЖТ
    90тЖТ```typescript
    91тЖТinterface MileageValidationRules {
    92тЖТ  // Format validation
    93тЖТ  maxFileSize: '10MB';
    94тЖТ  requiredColumns: ['train_number', 'reading_date', 'odometer_km'];
    95тЖТ  dateFormat: 'YYYY-MM-DD';
    96тЖТ  
    97тЖТ  // Business validation
    98тЖТ  maxDailyIncrease: 2000;           // km - anything more is anomaly
    99тЖТ  maxDailyDecrease: 0;              // Negative not allowed without meter event
   100тЖТ  duplicateHandling: 'UPDATE';       // UPDATE or REJECT
   101тЖТ  unknownTrainHandling: 'QUARANTINE'; // QUARANTINE or REJECT_FILE
   102тЖТ  
   103тЖТ  // Timing
   104тЖТ  expectedArrivalWindow: {
   105тЖТ    start: '05:00',                 // Local time
   106тЖТ    end: '07:00'
   107тЖТ  };
   108тЖТ  alertIfMissingAfter: '08:00';
   109тЖТ}
   110тЖТ```
   111тЖТ
   112тЖТ### 7.3.4 Error Handling
   113тЖТ
   114тЖТ```typescript
   115тЖТinterface IngestionError {
   116тЖТ  severity: 'WARNING' | 'ERROR' | 'CRITICAL';
   117тЖТ  code: string;
   118тЖТ  message: string;
   119тЖТ  row?: number;
   120тЖТ  field?: string;
   121тЖТ  value?: any;
   122тЖТ  action: 'SKIP_ROW' | 'QUARANTINE_FILE' | 'ALERT_ADMIN';
   123тЖТ}
   124тЖТ
   125тЖТconst ERROR_CODES = {
   126тЖТ  // File-level errors
   127тЖТ  'FILE_001': { severity: 'CRITICAL', message: 'File size exceeds limit' },
   128тЖТ  'FILE_002': { severity: 'CRITICAL', message: 'Invalid file format' },
   129тЖТ  'FILE_003': { severity: 'ERROR', message: 'Missing required columns' },
   130тЖТ  
   131тЖТ  // Row-level errors
   132тЖТ  'ROW_001': { severity: 'ERROR', message: 'Unknown train number' },
   133тЖТ  'ROW_002': { severity: 'WARNING', message: 'Future date not allowed' },
   134тЖТ  'ROW_003': { severity: 'WARNING', message: 'Negative daily mileage' },
   135тЖТ  'ROW_004': { severity: 'WARNING', message: 'Spike detected (>2000km/day)' },
   136тЖТ  'ROW_005': { severity: 'ERROR', message: 'Invalid odometer value' },
   137тЖТ  
   138тЖТ  // System errors
   139тЖТ  'SYS_001': { severity: 'CRITICAL', message: 'Database connection failed' },
   140тЖТ  'SYS_002': { severity: 'ERROR', message: 'Transaction rollback' }
   141тЖТ};
   142тЖТ```
   143тЖТ
   144тЖТ---
   145тЖТ
   146тЖТ## 7.4 Maximo Integration
   147тЖТ
   148тЖТ### 7.4.1 Integration Architecture
   149тЖТ

   151тЖТтФМтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФР         тФМтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФР         тФМтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФАтФР
